asBuilt.stockIntegration:
CONFIRMED: There is NO ConsumableStockBalance and NO decrementForIssue method anywhere. The doc's named dependency is a phantom — do not treat its exact names as real. The REAL stock-decrement seams are two parallel engines:

PHARMACY engine — pharmacy/application/StockService.java (the single owner of every PharmacyMedicine aggregate mutation). It is a @Service @Transactional bean, NOT in a published named-interface. Methods:
- decrementFefo(pharmacyUid, medicineUid, qty, MovementType, reference, ctx) -> List<ConsumedLot> (StockService.java:69-85): hard negative-stock gate FIRST (pm.decrement(qty) throws InsufficientStockException 422 at :78), then FEFO StockBatch walk + append-only StockMovement OUT row. Returns consumed lots for lot-trace.
- decrementAggregateOnly(...) (StockService.java:97-109): aggregate decrement + stock-card OUT row, NO batch walk (the OTC path).
- increment / receiveLot / overwriteStock / ensureOpeningStock for the IN side.

INVENTORY (store) engine — inventory/application/StoreStockService.java, the mirror for StoreItem: decrementFefo(storeUid, itemUid, qty, StoreMovementType, reference, ctx) -> List<ConsumedLot> (StoreStockService.java:83-95), same hard-gate-first pattern (si.decrement at :89).

CROSS-MODULE SEAM REALITY (load-bearing): The ONLY pharmacy type in a published @NamedInterface("api") is pharmacy/api/PharmacyStockCredit.java — and it is a CREDIT/transfer interface (creditTransferLot, creditTransferAggregate, debitTransferOut), NOT a general consumable-issue decrement. Its debitTransferOut(pharmacyUid, medicineUid, qty, reference, ctx) (PharmacyStockCredit.java:67-68) IS a published hard-gated decrement, but it is semantically labeled for pharmacy->pharmacy transfer-out and writes a TRANSFER_OUT card with a transfer-flavored reference. inventory/ has NO api package at all (the inventory<->pharmacy edge is inventory calling DOWN into pharmacy::api).

IMPLICATION FOR INC-07: There is currently NO published cross-module seam for an inpatient consumable issue to decrement stock. StockService.decrementFefo / StoreStockService.decrementFefo are @Service beans but package-private to pharmacy.application / inventory.application respectively (no NamedInterface), so inc-07 cannot legally call them across the Modulith boundary as-is. Inc-07 must either (a) define a NEW published seam (mirroring PharmacyStockCredit) exposing a CONSUMABLE_ISSUE-flavored decrementFefo, or (b) reuse debitTransferOut with caveats. Flag as an ambiguity/gap requiring an explicit seam decision — do NOT assume decrementForIssue exists.

PESSIMISTIC LOCK: NOT available. Verified the three PESSIMISTIC hits (StockService.java, PharmacyMedicineRepository.java:13-14, StockBatchRepository.java) are ALL comments documenting the lock is PARKED as CR-08-Q4 + ADR-0017. No @Lock/LockModeType annotation exists on any finder. Concurrency is guarded ONLY by inherited @Version on AuditableEntity (AuditableEntity.java:77-79). Inc-07 inherits the parked-lock posture.

This @Version is the ONLY concurrency guard (no pessimistic lock anywhere).
- Money (shared/domain/Money.java): @Embeddable VO, NUMERIC(19,2) HALF_UP, default TZS; of()/zero() factories.
- MoneyDto (shared/application/dto/MoneyDto.java) + MoneyMapper — wire form used by ChargeResult.

extract.lane: Admission lifecycle + ward/bed assignment + ward-to-ward transfer
extract.summary: Admission is a single flat JPA entity (`admissions` table) with `status` as a free-text String (NOT a typed enum). The lifecycle status values actually written are PENDING -> IN-PROCESS -> {STOPPED -> SIGNED-OUT} | {HELD -> SIGNED-OUT} (deceased). A bed is claimed via a one-to-one `Admission.wardBed` plus a separate `AdmissionBed` ledger row (status OPENED/CLOSED) carrying the per-period bed charge; the `WardBed.status` String cycles EMPTY -> WAITING -> OCCUPIED -> EMPTY. Critically, `doAdmission` creates the admission as PENDING with bed WAITING and only the INSURANCE-fully-covered path immediately flips to IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED; the CASH path (and insurance-with-topup path) stays PENDING until the ward-bed bill is PAID, at which point PatientBillResource.java:356-358 promotes the admission to IN-PROCESS and the bed to OCCUPIED. A 5-minute background thread (`UpdatePatient implements Runnable`, started in MainApplication) accrues a fresh ward-bed bill + new AdmissionBed every >=24h for IN-PROCESS/STOPPED admissions. There is NO WardTransfer entity and NO ward-to-ward transfer endpoint at all: the bed is fixed at admit time via `@JoinColumn(updatable=false)` and is never reassigned in-place anywhere; the only `*Transfer` domain class is ConsultationTransfer (outpatient routing, unrelated). Admit is blocked only by an existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission for the same patient and by a bed that is inactive or not EMPTY; a DECEASED patient is NOT explicitly blocked from admission.

findings.topic: Admission entity shape & status type
findings.behaviour: Admission is a flat entity mapped to table `admissions`; `status` is a free-text String (line 45), NOT a typed enum. Other fields: paymentType (@NotBlank String, comment lists CASH/DEBIT CARD/CREDIT CARD/MOBILE/INSURANCE), membershipNo (String), patient (@ManyToOne, not-null, updatable=false), visit (@OneToOne, not-null, updatable=false), insurancePlan (@ManyToOne nullable), wardBed (@OneToOne nullable, updatable=true), plus forensic createdBy/On/At, admittedBy/On/At, dischargedBy/On/At (discharged* nullable).

findings.topic: AdmissionBed entity (bed-charge ledger)
findings.behaviour: AdmissionBed (`admission_beds` table) is a per-period bed-occupancy/charge record with free-text String `status`, a mandatory @OneToOne PatientBill (the ward-bed charge), @ManyToOne wardBed (updatable=false), @ManyToOne admission, @ManyToOne patient, and openedAt/closedAt LocalDateTime. It is the ledger that backs ward-day billing; it is NOT a transfer record.

findings.topic: WardBed entity & status
findings.behaviour: WardBed (`ward_beds` table) has free-text String `status`, String `no`, boolean `active` (default false), @ManyToOne ward (updatable=false). New beds are created with status EMPTY and active=true; a ward enforces a hard bed-count cap (`count >= ward.noOfBeds` throws 'Bed/Room Limit reached').

findings.topic: Admit guard: existing open admission (HARD reject)
findings.behaviour: doAdmission controller hard-rejects if the patient already has any Admission in status PENDING or IN-PROCESS: throws InvalidOperationException 'Could not process admission. The patient is already admitted'. This is a service-layer reject, not a worklist filter.

findings.topic: Admit guard: bed must be active & EMPTY (HARD reject)
findings.behaviour: In PatientServiceImpl.doAdmission: if !wb.isActive() throws 'Could not process admission, bed not active'; if bed status != 'EMPTY' throws 'Could not process admission, bed already occupied. Please select a different bed.' On success the bed is immediately set to WAITING (not OCCUPIED yet).

findings.topic: Admit creates PENDING admission + WAITING bed + ward-bed PatientBill + AdmissionBed(OPENED)
findings.behaviour: doAdmission sets admission.status='PENDING', creates a new Visit (status PENDING, sequence 'SUBSEQUENT-FOR-ADMISSION'), creates an UNPAID PatientBill priced at ward.wardType.price (billItem 'Bed', description 'Ward Bed / Room'), creates AdmissionBed.status='OPENED' linking admission+patient+wardBed+bill, and flips patient.type='INPATIENT'. All in one @Transactional service call.

findings.topic: PENDING->IN-PROCESS activation differs by payment path
findings.behaviour: INSURANCE path: only the branch where the eligible plan fully covers (no top-up needed, line 1950 else) sets admission.status='IN-PROCESS' and wardBed.status='OCCUPIED' inside doAdmission. The CASH path and the insurance-with-topup path leave admission PENDING / bed WAITING at end of doAdmission (method returns at line 2020 without activating).

findings.topic: Payment-driven activation (CASH/topup admissions)
findings.behaviour: When a ward-bed bill is paid, the payment handler finds all the patient's PENDING admissions and sets each to IN-PROCESS and its wardBed to OCCUPIED, and signs out any IN-PROCESS consultations. This is the activation gate for non-fully-covered admissions.

findings.topic: No separate 'verify admission' endpoint
findings.behaviour: There is no verifyAdmission / verify_admission endpoint. PENDING->IN-PROCESS happens ONLY via (a) full-insurance-cover branch in doAdmission, or (b) ward-bed bill payment in PatientBillResource. NOT FOUND: searched PatientResource.java and PatientServiceImpl.java for 'verify_admission','verifyAdmission','setStatus("IN-PROCESS")'.

findings.topic: Discharge flow: save_discharge_plan sets STOPPED
findings.behaviour: saveDischargePlan: if no existing DischargePlan for the admission, it sets admission.status='STOPPED' and creates DischargePlan with status='PENDING'. STOPPED is the 'discharge initiated, awaiting bill clearance' state. The admission is NOT yet signed out and the bed is NOT yet freed here.

findings.topic: Discharge completion: get_discharge_summary -> SIGNED-OUT
findings.behaviour: getDischargeSummary loads all admission invoices; if any PatientBill is UNPAID or VERIFIED it HARD-rejects ('Patient have uncleared bills.'). Otherwise it APPROVES all invoices and, only if admission.status=='STOPPED', sets admission.status='SIGNED-OUT', stamps dischargedBy/On/At from the plan, sets wardBed.status='EMPTY', resets patient to type='OUTPATIENT', paymentType='CASH', insurancePlan=null, and DischargePlan.status='APPROVED'.

findings.topic: Referral flow (inpatient): STOPPED then SIGNED-OUT
findings.behaviour: saveReferralPlan for an admission sets admission.status='STOPPED' and ReferralPlan.status='PENDING'. getReferralSummary, after the uncleared-bills check (UNPAID or VERIFIED), approves invoices and (if admission STOPPED) sets admission='SIGNED-OUT', stamps discharged*, frees bed to EMPTY, sets patient.type='OUTPATIENT', and ReferralPlan='APPROVED'. Same terminal state as discharge.

findings.topic: Deceased flow: IN-PROCESS->HELD->SIGNED-OUT
findings.behaviour: saveDeceasedNote: if admission.status==null it defensively sets 'IN-PROCESS'; if 'IN-PROCESS' it sets admission.status='HELD' and immediately frees the bed (wardBed.status='EMPTY') even before summary approval; DeceasedNote.status='PENDING'. getDeceasedSummary: after uncleared-bills check, if admission.status=='HELD' sets 'SIGNED-OUT', frees bed EMPTY, sets patient.type='DECEASED', DeceasedNote='APPROVED'. Note: deceased path does NOT stamp dischargedBy/On/At on the admission.

findings.topic: Ward-day accrual background job EXISTS
findings.behaviour: UpdatePatient implements Runnable; run() loops forever with Thread.sleep(300000) (5 min). For each Admission in {IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}: if the open AdmissionBed is >=24h old (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now) >= 24) it sets that AdmissionBed.status='CLOSED'+closedAt, creates a NEW ward-bed PatientBill (status 'VERIFIED' for the new period) and a NEW AdmissionBed.status='OPENED'. Defensive de-dup: if >1 OPENED AdmissionBed exist it closes them and 'continue's. Started at boot in MainApplication.

findings.topic: Inpatient worklists are FILTERS by status (not gates)
findings.behaviour: getDoctorInpatientList returns admissions in {IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}; getNurseInpatientList returns only IN-PROCESS. So a PENDING (unpaid CASH) admission appears in NO inpatient worklist until payment promotes it to IN-PROCESS. These are pure read filters.

findings.topic: Nursing/charting gate on admission status
findings.behaviour: Inpatient charting operations (e.g. savePatientDressingChart) HARD-reject when admission.status=='PENDING' ('Admission not verified'), proceed only when 'IN-PROCESS', and reject otherwise ('Patient already signed off'). Confirms PENDING means not-yet-active.

findings.topic: Consultation blocked while patient has active admission
findings.behaviour: Outpatient consultation creation is hard-rejected when the patient has an admission in {PENDING, IN-PROCESS}: 'Could not process consultation, the patient has an active admission'. Cross-context guard preventing simultaneous inpatient+outpatient billing.

absences.expected: A typed Admission.status enum (e.g. AdmissionStatus)
absences.searched: domain/Admission.java line 45 — `private String status;` is a plain free-text String with no @Enumerated and no enum type. All transitions are string literals: PENDING, IN-PROCESS, STOPPED, HELD, SIGNED-OUT.

absences.expected: A WardTransfer entity / ward-to-ward transfer endpoint (transfer a patient's bed/ward in-place)
absences.searched: Glob of com/orbix/api/domain/*.java (full list) shows only ConsultationTransfer, no WardTransfer/BedTransfer; grep for change_bed|changeBed|transferWard|ward_transfer|reassign|setWardBed across com/orbix/api found NO reassignment path. Admission.wardBed and AdmissionBed.wardBed are both @JoinColumn(updatable=false). A new bed is only ever set at admit time in PatientServiceImpl.doAdmission (1718) and when the 24h accrual job opens a fresh AdmissionBed on the SAME ward bed (UpdatePatient.java:330). CONFIRMED ABSENT: legacy has no ward-to-ward or bed-to-bed transfer.

absences.expected: A separate MAR (Medication Administration Record) entity
absences.searched: Full domain/*.java listing — no MAR/MedicationAdministration entity. Inpatient drug administration is via PatientPrescriptionChart / Prescription.setStatus('GIVEN') (PatientResource.java:3230), not a dedicated MAR.

absences.expected: A FluidBalance entity
absences.searched: Full domain/*.java listing — no FluidBalance/IntakeOutput entity exists. CONFIRMED ABSENT.

absences.expected: A typed DischargePlan.kind / discharge-disposition classifier
absences.searched: DischargePlan, ReferralPlan, and DeceasedNote are three SEPARATE entities each with their own free-text String `status` (PENDING/APPROVED); there is no single DischargePlan.kind discriminator. Disposition is encoded by which of the three records is created, plus the resulting Patient.type (OUTPATIENT vs DECEASED).

absences.expected: Hibernate Envers audit trail (@Audited)
absences.searched: grep @Audited|Envers across com/orbix/api — NO files found. No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active in the legacy system: the dependency is present in pom.xml but no entity is annotated @Audited. Downstream agents must not assume an Envers audit baseline exists. (Forensic createdBy/On/At + admittedBy/On/At + dischargedBy/On/At columns on Admission are manual stamps, not Envers.)

absences.expected: Device-fingerprint / device-binding logic in security or admission flow
absences.searched: grep fingerprint|deviceId|device_id|deviceBinding (case-insensitive) across com/orbix/api — NO files found. No device-fingerprint or device-binding feature exists in the legacy system. Agents must not treat this as a feature to preserve or re-implement.

absences.expected: A guard blocking admission of a DECEASED patient
absences.searched: PatientResource.java:5189-5205 doAdmission only checks (a) patient exists, (b) no existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission, (c) bed exists; PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1709 only checks bed active+EMPTY. No check of patient.type=='DECEASED'. CONFIRMED ABSENT: a deceased patient is not explicitly blocked from re-admission at the admit path.

verdicts.planClaim: Admission is a typed state machine ADMITTED -> DISCHARGED / DECEASED / REFERRED / TRANSFERRED (entities/states named ADMITTED etc.).
verdicts.evidence: Admission.java:45 — `private String status;` is free-text String, no @Enumerated, no enum type. The actual literal lifecycle written by the code is PENDING -> IN-PROCESS -> {STOPPED -> SIGNED-OUT} (discharge/referral) | {HELD -> SIGNED-OUT} (deceased) (PatientResource.java:5259-5289, 5342-5385, 5446-5459/5610-5632, 5719-5732/5889-5908). None of the doc's state names (ADMITTED/DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED/TRANSFERRED) appear in legacy. Critically the doc collapses a TWO-PHASE lifecycle (PENDING-not-yet-active vs IN-PROCESS-active) into a single ADMITTED state, erasing the payment-gated activation that is the core admission rule. This is the same typed-state-over-untyped-system drift as inc-06.
verdicts.correction: Model the legacy two-phase lifecycle: PENDING (admission created, bed WAITING, ward-bed bill UNPAID, NOT yet on any worklist, charting rejected with 'Admission not verified') -> IN-PROCESS (active; reached either by the full-insurance-cover branch at admit OR by ward-bed bill payment) -> STOPPED (discharge/referral initiated) | HELD (deceased) -> SIGNED-OUT (terminal). A typed enum is an acceptable modern re-modelling ONLY if it preserves these five observable states and transitions; the names ADMITTED/DISCHARGED/etc. must map 1:1 to PENDING/SIGNED-OUT and the PENDING-vs-IN-PROCESS distinction must survive. TRANSFERRED must be removed entirely (see WardTransfer verdict).

verdicts.planClaim: Admission fields include billsCleared (denormalised flag owned by billing dispatcher) and businessDayId.
verdicts.evidence: Admission.java:38-84 has no billsCleared and no businessDayId/businessDay field. Bill clearance is NOT a stored flag: getDischargeSummary/getReferralSummary/getDeceasedSummary recompute it LIVE by loading all admission invoices and hard-rejecting if any PatientBill is UNPAID or VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:5342-5385). There is no 'billing dispatcher' writing a flag and no business-day concept on the admission. This is the inc-08-class invented denormalised flag.
verdicts.correction: Either preserve the legacy live-recompute (scan invoices for UNPAID/VERIFIED at discharge time) OR, if a denormalised bills_cleared flag is desired for the modular-monolith dispatcher pattern, raise it as a NET-NEW change request — it is not legacy behaviour. businessDayId has no legacy basis on Admission and should be dropped or CR'd.

verdicts.planClaim: Admission fields wardUid / bedUid / admittedAt / dischargedAt.
verdicts.evidence: Admission.java:38-84 carries wardBed (@OneToOne, updatable=true), admittedBy/On/At, and dischargedBy/On/At (nullable). So admittedAt/dischargedAt and a bed reference exist. Corrections: legacy has ONE wardBed relation, not separate wardUid+bedUid (ward is reached via WardBed.ward); and the deceased path does NOT stamp dischargedBy/On/At (PatientResource.java:5889-5908), so dischargedAt being null on a DECEASED/SIGNED-OUT admission is correct legacy behaviour, not a bug.
verdicts.correction: Keep admittedAt/dischargedAt and a bed reference (uid-based modern re-modelling of the wardBed relation is fine; derive ward from bed). Document that dischargedAt is NULL on the deceased terminal path (legacy stamps it only on discharge/referral). If the doc intends dischargedAt to always be set on terminal, that is a deviation requiring a CR.

verdicts.planClaim: WardBed has states FREE / OCCUPIED / RESERVED / OUT_OF_SERVICE; claimed (set OCCUPIED) on admit, released on terminal.
verdicts.evidence: WardBed.java:37-49 — status is a free-text String; legacy beds are created EMPTY (WardServiceImpl.java:132-143) and cycle EMPTY -> WAITING -> OCCUPIED -> EMPTY. RESERVED and OUT_OF_SERVICE do not exist in legacy. 'Claimed on admit' is wrong: doAdmission sets the bed to WAITING (PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1711), NOT OCCUPIED; OCCUPIED happens only later — at the full-insurance-cover branch (1950-1963) or on ward-bed bill payment (PatientBillResource.java:352-365). 'Released on terminal' is accurate (set EMPTY on SIGNED-OUT, and deceased frees the bed early at HELD).
verdicts.correction: Use the legacy four-value cycle EMPTY -> WAITING -> OCCUPIED -> EMPTY (a typed enum is fine if it maps 1:1). Admit sets WAITING (your FREE->RESERVED-ish intermediate), payment/full-cover sets OCCUPIED, terminal sets EMPTY. Drop RESERVED and OUT_OF_SERVICE unless raised as a NET-NEW CR (no legacy basis). Note the deceased flow frees the bed at the HELD stage (saveDeceasedNote, PatientResource.java:5719-5732), before summary approval — earlier than the discharge/referral paths.

verdicts.planClaim: WardBed updated on ward-to-ward transfer.
verdicts.evidence: CONFIRMED ABSENT. Admission.wardBed and AdmissionBed.wardBed are both @JoinColumn(updatable=false); grep for change_bed|changeBed|transferWard|ward_transfer|reassign across com/orbix/api found NO reassignment path. A bed is only ever set at admit time (PatientServiceImpl.java:1718) and re-opened on the SAME bed by the 24h accrual job (UpdatePatient.java:330). Legacy has no in-place bed/ward reassignment.
verdicts.correction: Remove ward-to-ward bed update from the WardBed lifecycle. If a transfer feature is desired, it is NET-NEW (CR) — see WardTransfer verdict.

verdicts.planClaim: WardTransfer — immutable record of every ward-to-ward move (prior/new ward+bed, reason, transferredAt) exists; endpoint POST /transfer-ward; state TRANSFERRED.
verdicts.evidence: CONFIRMED ABSENT. Glob of domain/*.java shows the only *Transfer class is ConsultationTransfer (outpatient routing, unrelated); there is no WardTransfer/BedTransfer entity and no ward-to-ward transfer endpoint. AdmissionBed exists but is the per-period BED-CHARGE ledger (status OPENED/CLOSED + a mandatory PatientBill), not a transfer record (AdmissionBed.java:40-68). Both bed FKs are updatable=false. This is a textbook inc-06/inc-08-class phantom (a modern feature idealized over a system that never had it).
verdicts.correction: Park the entire ward-to-ward transfer feature (WardTransfer entity, POST /transfer-ward endpoint, the TRANSFERRED state, and the 'WardBed updated on transfer' rule) as a NET-NEW change request in the change-request register with a clear NEW-SCOPE label. It must NOT carry acceptance criteria written as if it reproduces legacy behaviour. Note for legacy-analyst: do not confuse AdmissionBed (charge ledger) with a transfer record.

verdicts.planClaim: Deceased patient guard on admit() (DISCH-4): after DECEASED plan approval, Patient.deceased=true and admit()/book() reject the patient (422 PATIENT_DECEASED).
verdicts.evidence: CONFIRMED ABSENT. doAdmission guards only: patient exists, no existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission (PatientResource.java:5194-5200), and bed active+EMPTY (PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1709). There is NO check of patient.type=='DECEASED'. Legacy sets patient.type='DECEASED' at the deceased terminal (PatientResource.java:5889-5908) but never reads it to block re-admission or booking — a deceased patient is NOT explicitly blocked. There is also no boolean Patient.deceased flag in legacy (it is a 'type' string), and no 422/PATIENT_DECEASED error code.
verdicts.correction: This is a NET-NEW safety guard, not legacy reproduction. It is a sound and clinically defensible CR, but it must be logged in the change-request register as NEW SCOPE (legacy gap), not described as a fidelity 'fix'. The error code PATIENT_DECEASED and a boolean Patient.deceased flag are inventions — legacy uses patient.type='DECEASED' (a string). Acceptance criteria must state this is net-new. Also note the legacy DOES already block re-admission while a PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission is open and blocks outpatient consultation while {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} admission exists (PatientResource.java:531-533) — those existing guards should be preserved and not conflated with the new deceased guard.

newDriftFound: ACTIVATION GATE OMITTED (high risk): The doc's single ADMITTED state erases the legacy payment-gated activation. Legacy doAdmission creates the admission PENDING with bed WAITING and ONLY the full-insurance-cover branch (PatientServiceImpl.java:1950-1963) immediately activates to IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED; CASH and insurance-with-topup admissions stay PENDING until the ward-bed bill is PAID, at which point PatientBillResource.java:352-365 promotes ALL the patient's PENDING admissions to IN-PROCESS and beds to OCCUPIED (and signs out IN-PROCESS consultations). PENDING admissions appear in NO worklist (getDoctorInpatientList/getNurseInpatientList, PatientResource.java:3610-3633) and charting is hard-rejected with 'Admission not verified' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2062-2069). The doc has no PENDING/activation concept at all — this is a missing core safety/billing rule, not just naming drift.

WARD-DAY ACCRUAL JOB EXISTS IN LEGACY (contradicts the brief's expectation that it is NET-NEW): UpdatePatient implements Runnable, started in MainApplication.java:163-183, loops every 5 min (Thread.sleep(300000)); for each {IN-PROCESS,STOPPED} admission whose open AdmissionBed is >=24h old it CLOSES it, creates a NEW VERIFIED ward-bed PatientBill and a NEW OPENED AdmissionBed (UpdatePatient.java:104-110,258-334). So per-day ward-bed accrual is REAL legacy behaviour, NOT 'one flat charge' — the doc's exact-process target #2 is WRONG to claim 'legacy charges one flat amount at admission'. HOWEVER the legacy mechanism is a 24h-since-open elapsed-time loop with a VERIFIED-status new bill and defensive de-dup of multiple OPENED rows; it is NOT a midnight cron, NOT keyed on a business_date, and has NO (admission_uid, business_date) unique constraint / ON CONFLICT idempotency. The Spring @Scheduled midnight-cron + ShedLock + business-date unique-constraint model is a NET-NEW re-implementation (acceptable CR) but its idempotency/timing semantics differ from legacy (elapsed-24h vs calendar-midnight) and may produce a different number/timing of ward-day lines for a partial first/last day. This timing-semantics change must be a logged CR and the golden-master must reconcile elapsed-24h legacy output, not assume calendar nights.

AdmissionBed (the bed-charge ledger) is unrepresented in the doc and is load-bearing: it is a @OneToOne-to-PatientBill per-period record (status OPENED/CLOSED, openedAt/closedAt) that backs ward-day billing (AdmissionBed.java:40-68). The accrual idempotency and the 'discharge blocked by uncleared bills' rule both hinge on this ledger. The doc models accrual as InvoiceLine rows with no equivalent ledger entity, which is a modeling gap to flag for data-architect/data-migration (3 years of AdmissionBed rows must migrate and reconcile against PatientBill).

'billsCleared' check semantics drift: the doc says discharge is blocked while billsCleared=false set by a dispatcher when invoice balance reaches zero. Legacy blocks when ANY admission PatientBill is in status UNPAID OR VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:5342-5385) — note VERIFIED (an accrual-job-created status) also blocks, and the check APPROVES all invoices as a side effect on success. A simple 'balance == 0' flag does not capture the VERIFIED-blocks rule nor the approve-on-success side effect; reconcile these before writing acceptance criteria.

Self-approval / second-approver gate (M17, doc targets #3 and DoD) has NO legacy basis in the admission-lifecycle code reviewed: discharge/referral/deceased summaries are single-actor (createdBy stamps + dischargedBy on success); there is no approvedBy!=createdBy precondition and no SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN code anywhere in PatientResource discharge paths. Treat the two-approver discharge gate as NET-NEW scope (CR), not exact-process — flagged for the discharge-plan lane reviewer but noted here because it is asserted in the admission state-machine narrative (§52).

extract.summary: Three SEPARATE legacy entities handle end-of-encounter flows: DischargePlan (admission-only), ReferralPlan (admission OR consultation, with FK to ExternalMedicalProvider), and DeceasedNote (admission OR consultation). DischargePlan is ONE flat entity with all narrative fields (history/investigation/management/operationNote/icuAdmissionNote/generalRecommendation) and has NO typed `kind` discriminator {DISCHARGE,DECEASED,REFERRAL} — the three "kinds" are three distinct entities/tables, not one polymorphic entity. All three carry a free-text String `status` defaulting to "PENDING", advancing only to "APPROVED" (no enum; only those two values ever set). There is NO second-approver gate: the "approve" endpoints (get_discharge_summary / get_referral_summary / get_deceased_summary) set approvedBy = the plan's own createdBy, so approver always equals creator. Discharge/referral/deceased finalization IS blocked on unpaid bills — enforced inline by reading PatientBill.getStatus() against "UNPAID"/"VERIFIED" (admission/invoice path) or "UNPAID" (consultation path); there is NO billsCleared flag field anywhere. timeOfDeath/causeOfDeath live on DeceasedNote (causeOfDeath String + user-entered date/time fields); referral facility is a FK to the ExternalMedicalProvider provider entity, not free-text. ALL logic lives in the PatientResource controller (class-level @Transactional); none of it is in PatientServiceImpl.

findings.topic: DischargePlan is ONE flat entity, no kind discriminator
findings.behaviour: DischargePlan has flat String fields history, investigation, management, operationNote, icuAdmissionNote, generalRecommendation. There is NO `kind`/discriminator/@Enumerated field — grep for kind|discriminator|@Enumerated|enum returned no matches. The {DISCHARGE,DECEASED,REFERRAL} concept is modeled as THREE separate entities/tables (discharge_plans, referral_plans, deceased_notes), not one polymorphic entity.

findings.topic: DischargePlan.status is free-text String, default PENDING
findings.behaviour: status is declared `private String status = "PENDING";` — a free-text String, NOT a typed enum. Only values ever assigned in code are "PENDING" (on save) and "APPROVED" (on summary). No @Enumerated anywhere.

findings.topic: DischargePlan create flow + side effect on Admission
findings.behaviour: saveDischargePlan: looks up Admission by id (404 if missing). If a DischargePlan already exists for that admission (findByAdmission) it is reused/updated; otherwise a new one is created AND the Admission.status is immediately set to "STOPPED" and saved. createdBy/createdOn/createdAt set from userService/dayService only on first creation. All narrative fields copied from request body; status forced to "PENDING" on every save. No bill check at create time.

findings.topic: No second-approver gate (approvedBy == createdBy) — DISCHARGE
findings.behaviour: get_discharge_summary finalizes the plan: it sets p.status="APPROVED", and approvedBy = p.getCreatedBy(), approvedOn = p.getCreatedOn(), approvedAt = dayService.getTimeStamp(). The approver is ALWAYS copied from the creator — there is NO distinct second approver, no approvedBy != createdBy check anywhere.

findings.topic: Discharge IS blocked on unpaid bills (inline, no billsCleared flag)
findings.behaviour: get_discharge_summary loads PatientInvoice findAllByAdmission, iterates PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill; if any PatientBill.getStatus() equals "UNPAID" or "VERIFIED" it throws InvalidOperationException('Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.'). This is a HARD service reject, not a worklist filter. There is NO billsCleared/bills_cleared field — grep returned no files.

findings.topic: Discharge finalization state transitions + bed/patient resets
findings.behaviour: After bill gate passes: all admission invoices set status "APPROVED". Then ONLY if Admission.status == "STOPPED": Admission.status -> "SIGNED-OUT", dischargedBy/On/At copied from plan.createdBy/On/At; WardBed.status -> "EMPTY"; Patient.type -> "OUTPATIENT", Patient.paymentType -> "CASH", Patient.insurancePlan -> null. If admission status is not STOPPED, the admission/bed/patient are NOT changed but the plan is still saved.

findings.topic: Discharge worklist filter
findings.behaviour: load_discharge_list returns DischargePlans with status IN ("PENDING","APPROVED") via findAllByStatusIn. This is the cashier/payments worklist (component lives under pages/payments).

findings.topic: ReferralPlan entity shape + provider FK (NOT free-text)
findings.behaviour: ReferralPlan is its own entity (table referral_plans) with referringDiagnosis + same narrative fields + String status default "PENDING". Referral facility is a @ManyToOne FK to ExternalMedicalProvider (external_medical_provider_id, nullable=false) — a real provider entity (code/name/address/telephone/email/active), NOT free-text. ReferralPlan also has nullable admission and consultation @ManyToOne and a required patient FK.

findings.topic: ReferralPlan supports BOTH inpatient and outpatient
findings.behaviour: save_referral_plan requires the provider to exist (404), and enforces XOR between admission.id and consultation.id (throws if both set or both null). For admission path it sets Admission.status "STOPPED". For consultation path it FIRST runs unpaid-bill gates over LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription PatientBill.status=="UNPAID" (throws on any), then sets Consultation.status "SIGNED-OUT", Patient.paymentType "CASH", insurancePlan null. Reuses existing PENDING plan via findByAdmissionAndStatus / findByConsultationAndStatus.

findings.topic: Referral approve = no second approver; bill gate differs by path
findings.behaviour: get_referral_summary: admission path checks PatientInvoiceDetail->PatientBill against "UNPAID" OR "VERIFIED"; consultation path checks LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bills against "UNPAID" only. On pass: admission path (only if status==STOPPED) -> SIGNED-OUT, bed EMPTY, Patient.type OUTPATIENT; consultation path -> Consultation SIGNED-OUT, Patient.type OUTPATIENT. In BOTH, approvedBy/On copied from createdBy/On, approvedAt = timeStamp. No approver-distinct check.

findings.topic: DeceasedNote captures causeOfDeath + user-entered time/date of death
findings.behaviour: DeceasedNote (table deceased_notes) has String patientSummary, String causeOfDeath, LocalDate date (column date_), LocalTime time (column time_), String status default PENDING, nullable admission + consultation @OneToOne, required patient. In save_deceased_note the backend sets deceasedNote.setDate(note.getDate()) and setTime(note.getTime()) VERBATIM from the request body — time/date of death are USER-ENTERED (Angular posts this.date/this.time), not server clock.

findings.topic: DeceasedNote create flow + mandatory validation + status transitions
findings.behaviour: save_deceased_note rejects if patientSummary or causeOfDeath is empty (InvalidOperationException 'Summary and cause of death are missing'), and enforces admission/consultation XOR. Admission path: if status null -> IN-PROCESS then -> HELD (and WardBed -> EMPTY if present); consultation path: Consultation.status -> HELD. status forced PENDING. Note: the IN-PROCESS->HELD is set in the same call when status was null; the actual ward bed free happens here on the held transition.

findings.topic: Deceased approve = no second approver; sets Patient.type DECEASED
findings.behaviour: get_deceased_summary runs the unpaid-bill gate (PatientBill "UNPAID" OR "VERIFIED") over both admission and consultation invoices, then approves invoices. Only if Admission.status=="HELD" (or Consultation.status=="HELD"): status -> "SIGNED-OUT", WardBed -> EMPTY (admission only), Patient.type -> "DECEASED", and note.approvedBy/On copied from createdBy/On, approvedAt = timeStamp. No approver-distinct check.

findings.topic: All logic in controller, transactional, security disabled
findings.behaviour: All discharge/referral/deceased logic lives in PatientResource (class annotated @Transactional at line 193) — none of it is in PatientServiceImpl (grep for DischargePlan|DeceasedNote|ReferralPlan in PatientServiceImpl.java returned no matches). Every @PreAuthorize on these endpoints is COMMENTED OUT (e.g. //@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-A','PATIENT-C','PATIENT-U')")), so no method-level role gate is active.

findings.topic: Angular UI confirms cashier-side single-step approval
findings.behaviour: The 'approve' action getDischargeSummary() is in DischargeListComponent under pages/payments (cashier lane); it calls GET /patients/get_discharge_summary?discharge_plan_id= and immediately prints the discharge summary PDF. There is no separate approver login/second confirmation — a single window.confirm precedes the call.

absences.expected: A typed DischargePlan.kind discriminator {DISCHARGE,DECEASED,REFERRAL} on a single polymorphic entity
absences.searched: DischargePlan.java (read in full, 64 lines) — no kind/discriminator field. Grep kind|discriminator|@Enumerated|enum in DischargePlan.java returned no matches. Confirmed three SEPARATE entities/tables instead: discharge_plans (DischargePlan.java), referral_plans (ReferralPlan.java), deceased_notes (DeceasedNote.java).

absences.expected: A typed status enum on DischargePlan/ReferralPlan/DeceasedNote
absences.searched: All three entity files read fully; status is `private String status = "PENDING"` (free-text String). No @Enumerated. Only literal values assigned in PatientResource are "PENDING" and "APPROVED".

absences.expected: A second-approver gate (approvedBy != createdBy enforcement)
absences.searched: get_discharge_summary (PatientResource.java:5378-5381), get_referral_summary (:5627-5630,:5679-5681), get_deceased_summary (:5904-5926). In every case approvedBy/approvedOn are copied directly from createdBy/createdOn; no comparison, no distinct-user check anywhere.

absences.expected: A billsCleared boolean flag on Admission/DischargePlan
absences.searched: Grep billsCleared|bills_cleared across the whole Zana-HMIS-API tree returned no files. The unpaid-bill block is enforced inline by reading PatientBill.getStatus() against "UNPAID"/"VERIFIED" (PatientResource.java:5346-5348).

absences.expected: Discharge/referral/deceased business logic in PatientServiceImpl service layer
absences.searched: Grep DischargePlan|DeceasedNote|ReferralPlan|dischargePlan|deceasedNote|referralPlan in PatientServiceImpl.java returned no matches. All logic is inline in the PatientResource controller methods.

absences.expected: A method-level @PreAuthorize role gate on the discharge/referral/deceased endpoints
absences.searched: PatientResource.java lines around each @PostMapping/@GetMapping (5253,5292,5312,5329,5392,5536,5562,5578,5693,5777,5821,5837) — every @PreAuthorize is commented out. Only class-level @Transactional (line 193) is active. No active access-control annotation on these endpoints.

verdicts.planClaim: DischargePlan has a typed kind discriminator kind in {DISCHARGE, DECEASED, REFERRAL} on a single polymorphic entity (lines 16, 33, 105).
verdicts.evidence: Ground truth: there is NO typed kind/discriminator. The three 'kinds' are THREE SEPARATE legacy entities/tables: DischargePlan (discharge_plans, DischargePlan.java:38-44 — flat narrative fields history/investigation/management/operationNote/icuAdmissionNote/generalRecommendation, no @Enumerated/kind field; grep for kind|discriminator|@Enumerated|enum returned no matches), ReferralPlan (referral_plans, ReferralPlan.java:39-67), and DeceasedNote (deceased_notes, DeceasedNote.java:40-46). Modeling them as one polymorphic entity with a kind discriminator contradicts the legacy structure. This is the same inc-06/inc-08 'typed-state-asserted-over-untyped-system' trap. Note also the three legacy entities are NOT symmetric: ReferralPlan and DeceasedNote support BOTH admission AND consultation (outpatient) flows (PatientResource.java:5398-5530, :5699-5766), whereas the doc scopes all three to Admission only — a further structural mismatch.
verdicts.correction: Re-model as three aggregates (or one aggregate with three explicit sub-types) only as a deliberate, documented modernization that preserves observable behaviour. State plainly that legacy has three distinct tables (discharge_plans / referral_plans / deceased_notes), not one entity with a kind column. If a single polymorphic DischargePlan.kind is chosen for the modern build, log it as a CR (modern re-modelling, behaviour-preserving) and acknowledge that DECEASED and REFERRAL legacy flows also apply to OUTPATIENT consultations, not just admissions.

verdicts.planClaim: DischargePlan states PENDING -> APPROVED (line 16).
verdicts.evidence: Ground truth confirms the only two status values ever set are 'PENDING' (on save) and 'APPROVED' (on summary/approve) — DischargePlan.java:46 declares private String status = "PENDING". The transition direction is faithful. Correction needed only on typing: legacy status is a free-text String, NOT a typed enum, and the same applies to ReferralPlan and DeceasedNote.
verdicts.correction: Keep the PENDING -> APPROVED transition. Note that the legacy field is a free-text String defaulting to 'PENDING'; introducing a typed enum is an acceptable behaviour-preserving modernization but should be noted as such, with the constraint that only PENDING and APPROVED are valid values.

verdicts.planClaim: approvedBy must differ from createdBy — a second-approver gate (M17); self-approval returns 422 SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN (lines 16, 34, 68, 86, 105).
verdicts.evidence: Ground truth: there is NO second-approver gate, and the legacy behaviour is the EXACT OPPOSITE. In get_discharge_summary the approver is always copied from the creator: approvedBy = p.getCreatedBy(), approvedOn = p.getCreatedOn() (PatientResource.java:5378-5381). The same copy-from-creator pattern holds for referral (PatientResource.java:5627-5630, :5679-5681) and deceased (:5904-5926). The Angular UI confirms a single-step cashier-side approval with one window.confirm and no second login (discharge-list.component.ts:89-113). The absence search explicitly found no approvedBy != createdBy comparison anywhere. So in legacy approvedBy ALWAYS equals createdBy — requiring them to differ would forbid the one path the legacy actually executes.
verdicts.correction: Remove the second-approver gate from the legacy-reproduction scope. If a maker-checker / second-approver control is desired, it is NET-NEW scope: park it as a Change Request (a new clinical/financial control, not a legacy behaviour) requiring engagement-lead approval, and write its acceptance criteria as net-new, NOT as 'reproduces legacy'. The 'M17 missing-gate' framing is itself unfounded — legacy never had the gate, so it was never 'missing'.

verdicts.planClaim: DISCHARGE kind requires history, investigation, management, recommendations (line 16).
verdicts.evidence: Ground truth: DischargePlan.java:38-44 does carry the narrative fields (history, investigation, management, operationNote, icuAdmissionNote, generalRecommendation), BUT the legacy create flow does NOT enforce them as mandatory. saveDischargePlan copies all narrative fields from the request body and forces status to PENDING with no required-field validation and no bill check at create time (PatientResource.java:5259-5286). Asserting these as required validations is an invented constraint. (Contrast: DeceasedNote create DOES validate patientSummary + causeOfDeath as mandatory — PatientResource.java:5699-5766 — so a required-field rule exists there but not for DISCHARGE.) The field name 'recommendations' also drifts from the legacy 'generalRecommendation'.
verdicts.correction: Do not assert these four fields as required for DISCHARGE — legacy performs no such validation at create. Either drop the mandatory rule or raise it as a CR (new data-quality control). Align field naming to legacy: history, investigation, management, operationNote, icuAdmissionNote, generalRecommendation.

verdicts.planClaim: DECEASED kind requires timeOfDeath, causeOfDeath, and sets Patient.deceased = true; admit()/book() then reject the patient (PATIENT_DECEASED) (lines 16, 52, 82).
verdicts.evidence: Partially supported, partially invented. SUPPORTED: causeOfDeath is captured and validated mandatory along with patientSummary (DeceasedNote.java:40-46; save_deceased_note rejects empty patientSummary/causeOfDeath, PatientResource.java:5699-5766); the time/date of death are USER-ENTERED (note.getDate()/getTime() copied verbatim from the request body, not server clock). DRIFT: legacy does NOT set a boolean Patient.deceased flag — get_deceased_summary sets Patient.type = "DECEASED" (a String type field), not a deceased boolean (PatientResource.java:5889-5931). Grep for billsCleared-style flags and the entity reads show no deceased boolean. There is also no evidence of any PATIENT_DECEASED guard on admit()/book() in legacy — that re-book guard (DISCH-4) is asserted with no cited legacy basis and should be treated as net-new.
verdicts.correction: State that DECEASED finalization sets Patient.type = 'DECEASED' (string), matching legacy — not a new boolean Patient.deceased. timeOfDeath/causeOfDeath are mandatory and user-entered (preserve user-entered date/time, do not substitute server clock). The admit()/book() PATIENT_DECEASED re-book guard has no legacy basis cited; flag it as NEW SCOPE / CR (a safety improvement) rather than legacy reproduction, and confirm with legacy-analyst whether any legacy guard keys off Patient.type == 'DECEASED'. Also note DECEASED applies to consultation (outpatient) flows too, not just admissions.

verdicts.planClaim: REFERRAL kind requires referralFacilityUid as an FK to ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata, not free-text (gap DISCH-5) (lines 16, 78).
verdicts.evidence: Ground truth confirms exactly this: ReferralPlan.java:39-67 has a @ManyToOne FK to ExternalMedicalProvider (external_medical_provider_id, nullable=false) — a real provider entity (code/name/address/telephone/email/active), NOT free-text. save_referral_plan requires the provider to exist (404 if missing) (PatientResource.java:5398-5530). The 'DISCH-5 gap' framing (legacy used free-text) is itself wrong — legacy ALREADY uses the FK — but the planning-doc target (FK to ExternalMedicalProvider, reject free-text) faithfully reproduces legacy behaviour.
verdicts.correction: Claim is correct in substance. Minor: drop the 'gap DISCH-5 / legacy was free-text' premise — legacy already enforces the ExternalMedicalProvider FK (nullable=false), so this is reproduction, not a gap fix. Also note ReferralPlan supports both admission and consultation paths (XOR enforced), and the consultation path runs unpaid-bill gates over LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bills.

verdicts.planClaim: CASH discharge blocked if billsCleared = false; the billing dispatcher sets the billsCleared flag when the admission invoice balance reaches zero; approve() rejects with 409 ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING (lines 52, 70, 93, 106).
verdicts.evidence: The OUTCOME (discharge blocked on unpaid bills) is real, but the MECHANISM is invented. Legacy enforces the block INLINE in get_discharge_summary by loading PatientInvoice findAllByAdmission, iterating PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill, and throwing InvalidOperationException('...uncleared bills') if any PatientBill.getStatus() == 'UNPAID' OR 'VERIFIED' (PatientResource.java:5342-5352). There is NO billsCleared / bills_cleared field anywhere (grep across the whole Zana-HMIS-API tree returned no files), and therefore no billing dispatcher writing such a flag. The bill check is at APPROVE/summary time, not a denormalised flag maintained by a dispatcher. This is the same inc-08 pattern of idealizing toward a modern denormalised-flag + cross-module-dispatcher design that legacy does not have. (Also: legacy gates on bill status regardless of payment type within the admission path; the 'CASH-only' scoping is an added qualifier — the unpaid-bill throw is not conditioned on Patient.paymentType in the cited block.)
verdicts.correction: Reproduce the behaviour, not the invented mechanism: the discharge/referral/deceased approval must re-check actual bill status (any PatientBill in 'UNPAID' or 'VERIFIED' for the admission/invoice path; 'UNPAID' for the consultation path) at approval time and reject if any are uncleared. There is no legacy billsCleared flag. Introducing a denormalised billsCleared flag + billing dispatcher is a modern re-modelling — log as a CR and prove behavioural parity (reject iff any bill is uncleared). Confirm with legacy-analyst whether the block should be unconditioned by payment type (legacy throws on bill status irrespective of CASH/insurance in the cited path).

verdicts.planClaim: Approver worklist endpoint GET /inpatient/admissions/discharge-plans?status=PENDING returns only PENDING plans, pageable, gated by DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE privilege (DISCH-1) (lines 35, 90, 112).
verdicts.evidence: A worklist endpoint exists but its semantics differ. load_discharge_list returns DischargePlans with status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') via findAllByStatusIn (PatientResource.java:5317-5321) — it returns BOTH PENDING and APPROVED, not PENDING-only, and it is the CASHIER/payments worklist (the Angular component lives under pages/payments — discharge-list.component.ts:89-113), not a separate 'approver' worklist. Since there is no second-approver concept in legacy (approvedBy == createdBy), framing this as an 'approver worklist' for a distinct second approver is unfounded. The privilege code DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE is also invented: every @PreAuthorize on these endpoints is COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:193 class-level @Transactional only; e.g. //@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-A','PATIENT-C','PATIENT-U')")), so no role gate is active and no DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE authority exists in legacy.
verdicts.correction: Reproduce the legacy cashier worklist semantics: a list of discharge plans with status IN (PENDING, APPROVED), surfaced in the payments/cashier lane. The PENDING-only filter and the distinct 'second approver' framing are net-new (tied to the phantom second-approver gate) — park as CR. RBAC: legacy has NO active @PreAuthorize on these endpoints; if a DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE (or any) privilege is to gate this in the modern build, treat it as net-new RBAC hardening (acceptable per the audit/RBAC mandate) and confirm the actual privilege code with security-architect/legacy-analyst — the commented-out hints reference PATIENT-A/PATIENT-C/PATIENT-U, not DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE.

newDriftFound: Asymmetric scope: the doc treats DISCHARGE/DECEASED/REFERRAL as all-admission-only, but legacy ReferralPlan and DeceasedNote BOTH support outpatient consultation flows in addition to admission (XOR between admission.id and consultation.id; consultation path sets Consultation.status SIGNED-OUT/HELD, Patient.type OUTPATIENT, runs LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bill gates) — PatientResource.java:5398-5530, :5699-5766, :5589-5686. The doc omits the entire outpatient referral/deceased path.

Admission/bed/patient side-effect choreography is undocumented and conditional in legacy: create sets Admission.status='STOPPED'; discharge finalization only transitions to 'SIGNED-OUT' (and frees bed -> 'EMPTY', sets Patient.type='OUTPATIENT', paymentType='CASH', insurancePlan=null) IF Admission.status=='STOPPED' — otherwise the plan saves but nothing else changes (PatientResource.java:5259-5286, :5354-5385). Deceased uses an intermediate IN-PROCESS -> HELD transition and frees the bed on the HELD transition (PatientResource.java:5699-5766). The doc's simple ADMITTED -> DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED machine omits these STOPPED/HELD intermediate states and the legacy bed/patient resets.

Legacy state vocabulary differs from the doc: legacy terminal/intermediate admission statuses are STOPPED, SIGNED-OUT, IN-PROCESS, HELD and bed status EMPTY (not the doc's DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED/TRANSFERRED admission states and FREE/OCCUPIED/RESERVED/OUT_OF_SERVICE bed states). Confirm the modern enum mapping preserves these observable transitions; the doc's enum set is a re-modelling, not the legacy strings.

DeceasedNote uses split LocalDate (column date_) + LocalTime (column time_) user-entered fields (DeceasedNote.java:40-46), whereas the doc says timeOfDeath. Preserve that date and time are separate, user-entered values, not a single server-generated timestamp.

All discharge/referral/deceased logic lives in the PatientResource controller (class-level @Transactional, PatientResource.java:193), NOT in a service layer — none of it is in PatientServiceImpl. The doc's 'service-layer precondition' framing (line 86) is a modern re-layering; fine as modernization but note legacy had no service-layer split here.

extract.lane: Nursing charts — vitals, care plan, progress notes, dressing, fluid-balance / care-activity
extract.summary: In legacy Zana HMIS, the "nursing charts" lane is five flat JPA entities under com.orbix.api.domain, all wired to the same patient context via three nullable links (Consultation, NonConsultation, Admission) plus a Nurse. CRITICAL: there are NO separate FluidBalance or CareActivity entities — fluid balance (drainageOutput, fluidIntake, urineOutput) and care activity (feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar) are all plain String columns ON PatientNursingChart (PatientNursingChart.java:38-45). PatientNursingProgressNote is a single free-text note String with NO type/category enum (PatientNursingProgressNote.java:38); PatientNursingCarePlan is four free-text Strings (nursingDiagnosis, expectedOutcome, implementation, evaluation; PatientNursingCarePlan.java:38-41). PatientDressingChart has NO WoundStatus enum or wound-description field at all — it is essentially a billing record tied to a ProcedureType marked as a "dressing", carrying qty/paymentType/membershipNo and a mandatory PatientBill (PatientDressingChart.java:40-69); the masterdata Dressing entity is just a ProcedureType marker with no clinical fields (Dressing.java:35-43). Only PatientVital carries a status, and it is a FREE-TEXT String (default "EMPTY", values EMPTY/PENDING/SUBMITTED) — not a typed enum (PatientVital.java:45). Charts are editable/deletable only within a 24-hour window after createdAt (e.g. PatientResource.java:3135-3138); nursing chart/note/care-plan saves are pure inserts (append-only in practice, no update path), while vitals uniquely overwrites the single existing row. No @Audited/Envers and no device-fingerprint exist anywhere in com.orbix.api.

findings.topic: PatientVital entity field set + types
findings.behaviour: Flat entity, table patient_vitals. Fields all String: pressure, temperature, pulseRate, weight, height, bodyMassIndex, bodyMassIndexComment, bodySurfaceArea, saturationOxygen, respiratoryRate, description (length 1000). status is a FREE-TEXT String defaulting to "EMPTY" (NOT a typed enum). Links: @OneToOne consultation (nullable), @OneToOne nonConsultation (nullable), @ManyToOne admission (nullable). Audit cols createdBy/createdOn (Long, day id) + createdAt LocalDateTime. NO patient or nurse FK on PatientVital (unlike the other four charts).

findings.topic: FLUID BALANCE + CARE ACTIVITY location (CRITICAL)
findings.behaviour: There is NO separate FluidBalance or CareActivity entity. Both are plain nullable String columns on PatientNursingChart: care activity = feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar; fluid balance = drainageOutput, fluidIntake, urineOutput. All are free-text Strings (no numeric typing, no units enforced). One row = one combined nursing-chart snapshot.

findings.topic: PatientNursingChart links + audit
findings.behaviour: Table patient_nursing_charts. @ManyToOne to consultation/nonConsultation/admission (all nullable), @ManyToOne patient (nullable=false, updatable=false), @ManyToOne nurse (nullable). createdBy/createdOn Long + createdAt LocalDateTime. NO status field at all on this entity.

findings.topic: PatientNursingCarePlan entity field set
findings.behaviour: Table patient_nursing_care_plans. Four free-text String fields: nursingDiagnosis, expectedOutcome, implementation, evaluation. NO typed kind/category. Links: consultation/nonConsultation/admission (nullable), patient (nullable=false), nurse (nullable). Audit cols createdBy/createdOn/createdAt. NO status field.

findings.topic: PatientNursingProgressNote typing (CRITICAL)
findings.behaviour: Progress notes are NOT typed. The entity has a single free-text field 'note' (String). There is NO DOCTOR/NURSING/OBSERVATION/HANDOVER category enum or column. Table patient_nursing_progress_notes. Links: consultation/nonConsultation/admission (nullable), patient (nullable=false), nurse (nullable). Audit cols only.

findings.topic: PatientDressingChart entity + NO WoundStatus (CRITICAL)
findings.behaviour: Table patient_dressing_charts. There is NO WoundStatus enum and NO wound-description/clinical field. Fields: qty (double, @NotNull), paymentType (free-text String, comment lists CASH/DEBIT CARD/CREDIT CARD/MOBILE/INSURANCE), membershipNo (String). Mandatory FKs: procedureType (@ManyToOne, nullable=false), patientBill (@OneToOne, nullable=false). Nullable FKs: consultation/nonConsultation/admission, clinician, nurse, insurancePlan, patient (nullable=false). It is effectively a billing/charge record for a dressing-classified procedure, not a clinical wound chart. NO status field on the entity (the //dressingChart.setStatus("PENDING") line is commented out).

findings.topic: Dressing masterdata entity
findings.behaviour: Dressing (table dressings) is merely a marker that flags a ProcedureType as a billable dressing. It has only id, a @OneToOne procedureType, and audit cols. NO clinical/wound fields. savePatientDressingChart rejects if the ProcedureType is not listed in dressings ('Procedure type is not listed as dressing').

findings.topic: Vitals state machine (free-text status)
findings.behaviour: Status values are the literal strings EMPTY -> PENDING -> SUBMITTED. loadPatientVitalByConsultationId auto-creates a vital with status "EMPTY" if none exists (PatientResource.java:1298-1300). save_patient_vitals sets status "PENDING" on both update and create (PatientResource.java:1088,1107); HARD reject if existing status equals "SUBMITTED" ('Could not modify vitals. Vitals already submitted', :1074-1076). submit_patient_vitals: rejects if already SUBMITTED, sets SUBMITTED only when PENDING, else throws 'Operation failed' (:1180-1188). request_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id copies vital fields into a new GeneralExamination and requires status == SUBMITTED (:1322-1325).

findings.topic: Vitals are SINGLE-ROW upsert per context
findings.behaviour: save_patient_vitals loops findAllBy{Consultation|Admission|NonConsultation} and assigns the LAST element to 'vital' (last-wins); if found it overwrites all fields in place, else inserts a new row. So vitals are effectively one mutable record per context, NOT append-only.

findings.topic: Context exclusivity gate (vitals)
findings.behaviour: savePatientVitals/submitPatientVitals load Consultation+NonConsultation+Admission by id from the request body and HARD-reject any combination where more than one is present ('Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously'), and reject when all three are empty ('No Admission or Consultation or Non Consultation found').

findings.topic: Nursing chart / note / care plan are admission-only inserts
findings.behaviour: All three save methods (savePatientNursingChart/ProgressNote/CarePlan) share identical guards: require exactly one of c/nc/a present; HARD-reject consultation ('Operation not available for outpatients') and nonConsultation ('Operation not available for outsiders'); so in practice only Admission context is allowed. Require Nurse present ('Nurse information not found'). Each is a plain repository.save (insert) — NO update/edit path and NO status field, so they are append-only via the API.

findings.topic: Admission-status gate on nursing saves (HARD reject)
findings.behaviour: For Admission context, all three nursing saves + dressing save HARD-reject: status "PENDING" -> 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'; status "IN-PROCESS" -> allowed; any other status (e.g. signed off) -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off'. Admission.status is checked as a free-text String via .equals().

findings.topic: Dressing chart save = billing side-effects
findings.behaviour: savePatientDressingChart is the only nursing-lane save that writes money. It creates a PatientBill (billItem 'Procedure', description 'Dressing: '+name, status UNPAID, amount=ProcedureType.price). If patient.paymentType=="INSURANCE" OR admission present: looks up ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan(covered=true); if found sets bill COVERED/INSURANCE and adds a PatientInvoiceDetail under a PENDING PatientInvoice (creating one with no = String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with id). Else if admission present, bill status VERIFIED added to a null-insurance PENDING invoice. NO stock/inventory movement occurs (dressing consumes ProcedureType pricing only, not a batch).

findings.topic: 24-hour deletion window (all nursing charts)
findings.behaviour: deleteNursingChart, deleteNursingProgressNote, deleteNursingCarePlan, deleteDressingChart each compute ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(createdAt, now) and HARD-reject if >= 24 ('Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted'). Nursing chart/note/care-plan deletes are hard repository.delete with no billing reversal. Dressing delete additionally: raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists, deletes the linked PatientInvoiceDetail (and the PatientInvoice if it becomes empty), deletes the PatientPaymentDetail, then deletes the chart and its PatientBill.

findings.topic: Load (worklist) endpoints are unfiltered fetch-by-context
findings.behaviour: GET nursing_charts / nursing_progress_notes / nursing_care_plans and dressing-chart loads simply fetch findAllBy{Consultation|NonConsultation|Admission} with NO status filtering or gating — they return all rows for the context mapped to *Model DTOs. The 'created' display string concatenates createdAt + the creating user's nickname. So read access is a plain fetch, not a filtered worklist.

findings.topic: Audit-trail status (early-discovery obligation)
findings.behaviour: No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active. Grep for @Audited|Envers across com.orbix.api returned no files. No nursing entity is @Audited; the only audit is the manual createdBy/createdOn/createdAt columns. Downstream agents must not assume an Envers audit baseline exists for nursing charts.

findings.topic: Device-fingerprint / device-binding (early-discovery obligation)
findings.behaviour: No device-fingerprint or device-binding feature exists. Grep for fingerprint|deviceId|device_id|deviceBinding|userAgent across com.orbix.api returned no files. Nursing-chart endpoints rely only on the JWT-derived user (userService.getUser(request)) for createdBy. Agents must not treat device-binding as a feature to preserve.

absences.expected: A separate FluidBalance entity (intake/urine/drainage as its own table/record)
absences.searched: Glob domain/*FluidBalance*, grep FluidBalance|fluidBalance across com.orbix.api (no matches). Fields drainageOutput/fluidIntake/urineOutput are String columns on PatientNursingChart.java:43-45 instead.

absences.expected: A separate CareActivity entity (feeding/repositioning/blood-sugar as its own record)
absences.searched: Glob domain/*Chart* + grep CareActivity|careActivity across com.orbix.api (no matches). feeding/changingPosition/bedBathing/randomBloodSugar/fullBloodSugar are String columns on PatientNursingChart.java:38-42.

absences.expected: A WoundStatus enum (or typed wound-status / wound-description field) on dressing
absences.searched: grep WoundStatus|woundStatus across com.orbix.api (no matches); read PatientDressingChart.java:40-95 and Dressing.java:35-49 in full — no wound/clinical field exists; dressing chart is a billing record only.

absences.expected: Typed progress-note categories (DOCTOR/NURSING/OBSERVATION/HANDOVER)
absences.searched: Read PatientNursingProgressNote.java in full — only a single free-text 'note' String (line 38); no type/category enum or column anywhere.

absences.expected: A typed PatientVital.status enum (PatientVitalStatus)
absences.searched: Glob found PatientVitalStatus.java only in the NEW codebase (backend\...\clinical\domain), NOT in legacy com.orbix.api. Legacy PatientVital.status is a free-text String defaulting to "EMPTY" (PatientVital.java:45).

absences.expected: A MAR / MedicationAdministration entity in this lane
absences.searched: grep MAR|MedicationAdministration across com.orbix.api (no matches). Medication-to-patient charting lives in PatientPrescriptionChart / PatientConsumableChart (separate lane), not in the nursing-chart entities reviewed here.

absences.expected: Edit/update endpoints or an immutability flag for nursing chart/note/care-plan
absences.searched: grep + read of PatientServiceImpl save methods (2593-2751) and PatientResource save/delete (2203-2252, 3127-3179): only create + 24h-window delete exist; no update path and no status/locked column — effectively append-only-then-deletable-within-24h, not enforced immutable.

absences.expected: An audit-trail (Envers @Audited) or device-binding on nursing entities
absences.searched: grep @Audited|Envers and fingerprint|deviceId|deviceBinding|userAgent across com.orbix.api (no matches) — both confirmed ABSENT per early-discovery obligation.

verdicts.planClaim: SIX separate nursing chart aggregates exist: AdmissionVitalsEntry, NursingCarePlanItem, NursingProgressNote, DressingChartEntry, FluidBalanceEntry, CareActivityEntry (lines 17-23, 107).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has only FIVE flat JPA entities in this lane: PatientVital, PatientNursingChart, PatientNursingCarePlan, PatientNursingProgressNote, PatientDressingChart (all under com.orbix.api.domain). FluidBalanceEntry and CareActivityEntry are NOT separate entities — they are plain String columns ON PatientNursingChart (PatientNursingChart.java:38-45, confirmed by direct read: feeding/changingPosition/bedBathing/randomBloodSugar/fullBloodSugar + drainageOutput/fluidIntake/urineOutput). Glob domain/*FluidBalance* and grep CareActivity returned no matches. The 'six aggregates' framing is the same typed-entity inflation seen in inc-06.
verdicts.correction: Model as FIVE legacy charts. Fluid-balance and care-activity are fields of the combined PatientNursingChart snapshot, not first-class aggregates. A modern re-modelling MAY split them into separate tables/value objects ONLY as an explicit, CR-logged design decision (NEW SCOPE re-modelling), and acceptance criteria must not assert they reproduce a legacy entity. Behaviour to preserve: one combined nursing-chart row carries both fluid and care-activity values as free-text.

verdicts.planClaim: FluidBalanceEntry — intake / urine / drainage in mL per entry; shift totals derived at query time (ADMIT-1 fix); 'added late in V72' and must be first-class.
verdicts.evidence: No FluidBalance entity exists (Glob/grep no matches). drainageOutput/fluidIntake/urineOutput are nullable free-text String columns on PatientNursingChart.java:43-45 — NOT numeric, NO mL unit enforcement, NO per-entry record separate from the nursing chart, NO shift-total aggregation anywhere in legacy. 'mL numeric typing', 'shift totals derived at query time', and 'first-class entity' are all net-new modern design, not legacy behaviour. The 'added late in V72' provenance claim is unverifiable from the entity source and reads as narrative justification.
verdicts.correction: Reclassify FluidBalanceEntry as NEW SCOPE (CR). If retained, acceptance criteria must state it is a net-new re-modelling of legacy String columns (drainageOutput/fluidIntake/urineOutput on the nursing chart) — NOT a reproduction. Numeric mL typing and derived shift totals are improvements requiring CR approval; legacy stored free-text only. Do not assert 'first-class entity' as a legacy fidelity target.

verdicts.planClaim: CareActivityEntry — type enum (FEEDING, REPOSITIONING, BED_BATH, BLOOD_SUGAR, OTHER) + value/notes + recordedAt (ADMIT-2 fix); first-class.
verdicts.evidence: No CareActivity entity exists (grep CareActivity|careActivity no matches). The activities are five fixed free-text String columns on PatientNursingChart.java:38-42 (feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar) — there is NO type enum, NO generic value/notes structure, and NO OTHER category. Legacy has a fixed column-per-activity shape, not a typed (type, value) entity. The proposed enum is invented; note legacy distinguishes randomBloodSugar vs fullBloodSugar (two columns), which the single BLOOD_SUGAR enum value loses.
verdicts.correction: Reclassify CareActivityEntry as NEW SCOPE (CR). If retained as a re-model, acceptance criteria must say so and must map to the five legacy String columns. The CareActivityType enum is net-new; 'REPOSITIONING' maps to changingPosition, 'BED_BATH' to bedBathing, and BLOOD_SUGAR must NOT collapse the legacy random/full distinction without a CR. Park the enum as a design proposal, not legacy reproduction.

verdicts.planClaim: NursingProgressNote has kind ∈ {DOCTOR, NURSING, OBSERVATION, HANDOVER} (line 19).
verdicts.evidence: PatientNursingProgressNote.java:38 (direct read confirmed) has a single free-text field 'note' (String) and NO type/category column. grep for the four category values returns nothing in the entity. The typed 'kind' enum has no legacy basis whatsoever — it is invented, exactly the inc-06 'typed states asserted over a system that didn't implement them' pattern.
verdicts.correction: Remove the kind enum. Model NursingProgressNote as a single free-text note per the legacy entity. If categorised progress notes are clinically desired, raise a CR as NEW SCOPE; acceptance criteria must not claim it reproduces legacy. Also note: legacy progress-note saves are append-only inserts deletable only within 24h (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751; PatientResource.java:2967-3179), not a typed worklist.

verdicts.planClaim: DressingChartEntry is immutable with a WoundStatus enum (CLEAN, HEALING, GRANULATING, SLOUGHY, INFECTED, NECROTIC, DEHISCED) + dressing applied (line 20).
verdicts.evidence: PatientDressingChart.java:40-95 (direct read confirmed) has NO WoundStatus enum and NO wound/clinical field of any kind. It is a billing record: qty (double, @NotNull), paymentType (free-text), membershipNo, a mandatory @OneToOne patientBill (nullable=false) and mandatory @ManyToOne procedureType (nullable=false). The masterdata Dressing entity (Dressing.java:35-49) is merely a marker flagging a ProcedureType as billable, with no clinical fields. The 7-value WoundStatus clinical enum is entirely invented. Also note savePatientDressingChart writes money/bills (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245) — the doc treats dressing as a pure clinical chart and omits the billing side-effect.
verdicts.correction: Remove the WoundStatus enum (PHANTOM, CR-park if clinically wanted as NEW SCOPE). Model DressingChartEntry per legacy: a billing/charge record for a dressing-classified ProcedureType, with mandatory PatientBill creation, qty, paymentType, optional insurancePlan, and the 'Procedure type is not listed as dressing' guard. Acceptance criteria MUST capture the billing side-effects (UNPAID bill, INSURANCE→COVERED + PatientInvoiceDetail under a PENDING invoice when paymentType=INSURANCE or admission present; VERIFIED bill otherwise) and the credit-note reversal on delete. 'Immutable' is also imprecise — legacy allows hard delete within 24h with billing reversal (PatientResource.java:2967-3179).

verdicts.planClaim: NursingCarePlanItem — per-problem goal + intervention + evaluation; states ACTIVE → RESOLVED / CANCELLED (line 18).
verdicts.evidence: PatientNursingCarePlan (PatientNursingCarePlan.java:38-41) has four free-text Strings: nursingDiagnosis, expectedOutcome, implementation, evaluation. There is NO status field and NO ACTIVE/RESOLVED/CANCELLED lifecycle (confirmed by ground-truth findings; entity has no status column). The legacy is ONE care-plan snapshot record (append-only insert, deletable within 24h via PatientResource.java:2967-3179), not a per-problem item with a state machine. The 'per-problem item' + lifecycle framing is invented.
verdicts.correction: Remove the ACTIVE→RESOLVED/CANCELLED state machine (no legacy basis — DRIFT/CR-park). Model the care plan as a single record with four free-text fields (nursingDiagnosis, expectedOutcome, implementation, evaluation). The legacy field names should drive the DTO. If a per-problem lifecycle is desired, raise a CR as NEW SCOPE.

verdicts.planClaim: AdmissionVitalsEntry — immutable, append-only series: BP systolic/diastolic, temperature, pulse, respiration, SpO2; recorded per shift/round (line 17). Endpoint 'POST /vitals — append vitals entry' + 'GET /vitals — full vitals series'.
verdicts.evidence: Legacy PatientVital is a SINGLE-ROW upsert per context, NOT an append-only series. save_patient_vitals takes the last row found and overwrites all fields in place; only inserts if none exists (PatientResource.java:1043-1114), so there is at most one mutable vitals record per context — there is no 'series'. Field shape also differs: legacy has separate 'pressure' (single String, not split systolic/diastolic), plus weight/height/bodyMassIndex/bodyMassIndexComment/bodySurfaceArea/saturationOxygen/respiratoryRate, all free-text String (PatientVital.java:33-45). Vitals also carry a free-text status (EMPTY→PENDING→SUBMITTED) with a submit gate and a 'cannot modify once SUBMITTED' hard reject (PatientResource.java:1073-1192) — the doc's 'immutable append-only' framing omits the actual upsert+submit state machine.
verdicts.correction: Correct to a single mutable vitals record per context with the EMPTY→PENDING→SUBMITTED free-text status lifecycle (auto-create EMPTY on load; PENDING on save; reject modify when SUBMITTED; submit only from PENDING; request copies into GeneralExamination requiring SUBMITTED). 'pressure' is a single field (splitting into systolic/diastolic is a re-model needing a CR). If an append-only per-shift series is desired clinically, that is NEW SCOPE (CR) — legacy keeps one overwritten row. Acceptance criteria must reflect the upsert + submit gate, not 'immutable series'.

verdicts.planClaim: MedicationAdministration (MAR) aggregate is mandatory in this lane / nursing context (lines 21, 41-42, 88, 108).
verdicts.evidence: grep MAR|MedicationAdministration across com.orbix.api returned no matches — there is no MAR entity in legacy at all, and specifically not in the nursing-chart lane. The ground-truth notes medication-to-patient charting lives in PatientPrescriptionChart/PatientConsumableChart (a separate lane), not the nursing entities. MAR as described (administeredAt, doseGiven, routeUid, patientResponse, prescription-line link) is net-new. The doc's M15 framing ('prior build tracked only pharmacy dispensedAt; MAR mandatory') confirms MAR is an addition, not legacy reproduction.
verdicts.correction: Label MedicationAdministration/MAR as NEW SCOPE in the change-request register; acceptance criteria must state it is net-new, not a legacy reproduction. Any legacy-fidelity claim is false — legacy has no MAR. Confirm with legacy-analyst whether the intended source behaviour is PatientPrescriptionChart/PatientConsumableChart (different lane) before writing 'reproduces' criteria.

verdicts.planClaim: Nursing charts touch Consultation / NonConsultation / Admission contexts and the doc routes all chart endpoints as /inpatient/admissions/uid/{uid}/... (admission-only).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy entities link to all three contexts via nullable FKs (PatientNursingChart.java:47-60 etc.), but the save services HARD-reject consultation ('Operation not available for outpatients') and nonConsultation ('Operation not available for outsiders'), so in practice nursing chart/note/care-plan saves are Admission-only (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751). The doc scoping these under /inpatient/admissions is therefore behaviourally consistent. HOWEVER vitals are NOT admission-only — vitals accept consultation, nonConsultation OR admission with a context-exclusivity gate (PatientResource.java:1028-1071), so the vitals endpoint being modelled purely under /inpatient/admissions narrows legacy scope.
verdicts.correction: Keep admission-only scope for nursing chart/note/care-plan/dressing (matches legacy hard rejects). For VITALS, note legacy supports consultation/non-consultation/admission contexts with a mutual-exclusivity guard ('Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously') — restricting vitals to admission only is a scope reduction that should be a conscious CR decision, not silent. Also add the admission-status gate (PENDING→'not verified' reject; IN-PROCESS allowed; signed-off→'already signed off' reject; PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635) to the chart-write acceptance criteria.

verdicts.planClaim: Audit events emitted for every state transition and captured in audit_event (DoD line 114); broader audit-trail baseline assumed for nursing charts.
verdicts.evidence: grep @Audited|Envers across com.orbix.api returned no matches — no Hibernate Envers and no nursing entity is audited. The only legacy audit is manual createdBy/createdOn/createdAt columns. Per the standing engagement principle this is NET-NEW (tamper-evident audit is an approved net-new obligation, not a legacy behaviour to port). The claim is fine as a forward requirement but must NOT be framed as reproducing legacy audit.
verdicts.correction: Keep audit_event as a NET-NEW requirement (already CR-blessed per engagement audit-trail mandate), but explicitly tag it net-new in the story — do not imply nursing charts had an audit trail. Legacy preserved only createdBy/createdOn/createdAt. No device-fingerprint/device-binding exists either (grep no matches) — must not be ported.

newDriftFound: Dressing chart billing side-effects are entirely omitted from the doc. Legacy savePatientDressingChart (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245) creates a PatientBill (billItem 'Procedure', amount=ProcedureType.price, status UNPAID), and for INSURANCE patients or admissions looks up ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan(covered=true) -> sets COVERED/INSURANCE and adds a PatientInvoiceDetail under a PENDING PatientInvoice (else VERIFIED on a null-insurance invoice). The doc treats DressingChartEntry as a pure clinical chart with a WoundStatus enum, missing the actual financial behaviour entirely. This is the load-bearing legacy behaviour for dressings.

24-hour edit/delete window is undocumented. ALL nursing charts (chart/note/care-plan/dressing) are editable/deletable ONLY within 24h of createdAt and HARD-reject otherwise ('only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted'; PatientResource.java:2967-3179, 3135-3138). The doc asserts charts are 'immutable'/'append-only' which contradicts the actual 24h-mutable-then-locked window. Dressing delete additionally raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote, deletes the PatientInvoiceDetail/PatientPaymentDetail and the PatientBill — a billing reversal flow the doc omits.

Vitals submit state machine omitted. The free-text EMPTY->PENDING->SUBMITTED lifecycle with submit_patient_vitals (only PENDING->SUBMITTED), the 'Vitals already submitted' modify-reject, and request_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id copying fields into GeneralExamination requiring SUBMITTED (PatientResource.java:1073-1325) are entirely absent from the doc, which models vitals as a plain append-only series.

Context-exclusivity gate omitted. Legacy hard-rejects when more than one of consultation/nonConsultation/admission is present ('Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously') and when all three are empty ('No Admission or Consultation or Non Consultation found') for vitals (PatientResource.java:1028-1071). No equivalent invariant appears in the doc.

Admission-status gate on chart writes omitted.
