For admission context, chart/note/care-plan/dressing saves hard-reject Admission.status 'PENDING' ('Admission not verified'), allow 'IN-PROCESS', and reject any signed-off status ('Patient already signed off') via free-text .equals() (PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635). This precondition is missing from all nursing-chart acceptance criteria in the doc.

Nurse-required guard omitted. All three nursing saves require a Nurse to be present ('Nurse information not found'; PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751). The doc's chart aggregates do not capture this mandatory nurse linkage as a validation rule.

Legacy 'patient' linkage on charts is non-null and non-updatable (PatientNursingChart.java:62-64 etc.), but PatientVital uniquely has NO patient or nurse FK (PatientVital.java has only consultation/nonConsultation/admission + audit cols). The doc's uniform 'admission-scoped' chart modelling should account for this asymmetry (vitals are context-linked, the other four are patient+nurse-linked).

extract.summary: There is NO dedicated Medication Administration Record (MAR) / MedicationAdministration / per-administered-dose entity in the legacy Zana HMIS. A whole-repo scan for "MAR", "MedicationAdmin", "administer", "dispens", "drugChart", "medicationChart" returns zero application matches (the only "administ" hits are "system administrator" comment text; the only "fingerprint" hits are vendor JS libs). The CLOSEST artefact to a MAR is PatientPrescriptionChart (table patient_prescription_charts): a nurse free-texts dosage/output/remark against an admission's already-dispensed Prescription. It is a flat note record — it has NO status column, NO scheduled-dose / due / held / missed / refused concept, and NO administered-at timestamp distinct from the row's createdAt. The word "GIVEN" in the codebase does NOT mean "administered to patient"; it is the free-text status set on Prescription / PharmacySaleOrderDetail when the PHARMACY ISSUES/dispenses the drug (issueMedicine, PatientResource.java:3230 and 6259). Inpatient medication tracking therefore consists of three loosely-linked layers only: (1) Prescription order + its String status lifecycle and pharmacy issue/dispense, (2) PatientPrescriptionChart nurse dosing notes, and (3) PatientConsumableChart for billed consumables — none of which is a true closed-loop MAR. Confirmed phantom: no Envers @Audited on any chart entity; no device-binding. So administration of each dose is NOT auditably tracked as discrete dose events in legacy.

findings.topic: No MedicationAdministration/MAR entity exists anywhere
findings.behaviour: Whole-repo grep (case-insensitive) for MedicationAdministration|MAR|MedAdmin|administer|drugChart|medicationChart across D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature (backend + Angular) returns zero application files. No such domain class, repository, model, controller endpoint, or Angular interface exists.

findings.topic: PatientPrescriptionChart is the closest thing to a MAR; it is a free-text note, not a dose event
findings.behaviour: Entity has ONLY: id (IDENTITY), dosage:String, output:String, remark:String, plus ManyToOne to consultation/nonConsultation/admission/prescription(NotNull)/patient/clinician/nurse, plus createdBy:Long, createdOn:Long(dayId), createdAt:LocalDateTime. There is NO status field, NO 'administered' flag, NO scheduled-time/due-time, NO route/frequency on the chart itself (those live on Prescription), NO separate administeredAt distinct from createdAt. dosage/output/remark are all unconstrained String (no enum, no validation annotations).
findings.cite: PatientPrescriptionChart.java:34-82 (fields lines 38-40: dosage, output, remark; createdAt line 81)

findings.topic: Recording a dose = saving a PatientPrescriptionChart row; INPATIENT-ONLY; gated on prescription dispensed
findings.behaviour: savePatientPrescriptionChart requires a Nurse (throws 'Nurse information not found' if absent), requires the linked Prescription.status to equal 'GIVEN' else throws 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy', explicitly throws 'Operation not available for outpatients' for consultation and 'Operation not available for outsiders' for nonConsultation (so only admission path is allowed), and requires admission.status to be IN-PROCESS (PENDING->'Admission not verified'; any other->'Patient already signed off'). It then stamps createdBy/createdOn/createdAt and saves. NO stock movement, NO charge, NO Prescription mutation happens here — it is purely a note write.
findings.cite: PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590 (nurse check 2536-2538; GIVEN gate 2544-2546; outpatient/outsider rejects 2564-2569; admission status gate 2570-2577; save 2589)

findings.topic: 'GIVEN' means pharmacy-dispensed, NOT nurse-administered-to-patient
findings.behaviour: In issueMedicine, a Prescription with status 'NOT-GIVEN' is flipped to 'GIVEN' when the PHARMACY issues it; the same method sets issued/balance, issuePharmacy, approvedBy/approvedOn/approvedAt and decrements PharmacyMedicine stock. The identical pattern flips PharmacySaleOrderDetail to 'GIVEN' on pharmacy issue. So 'GIVEN' is a dispense/issue state in the pharmacy lane, never an administration-to-patient event.
findings.cite: PatientResource.java:3217-3237 (Prescription NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN on issue, sets issuePharmacy/approved* + stock check at 3243); PatientResource.java:6242-6264 (PharmacySaleOrderDetail NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN on issue)

findings.topic: Prescription order lifecycle is a free-text String, not a typed enum
findings.behaviour: Prescription.status is a plain String. Observed values set in code: 'NOT-GIVEN' (initial, PatientServiceImpl.java:1532) and 'GIVEN' (after pharmacy issue). Prescription also carries issued/balance doubles and a full set of *by/*On(dayId)/*At(LocalDateTime) audit-ish stamp triples for ordered/accepted/held/rejected/verified/approved, plus rejectComment — but these are pharmacy-workflow stamps, none represents per-dose administration.

findings.topic: PatientConsumableChart is a billing/charge chart with its own String status, not administration
findings.behaviour: PatientConsumableChart has qty:double(NotNull), status:String, paymentType:String, membershipNo:String, a OneToOne to PatientBill (NotNull), plus medicine/insurancePlan/nurse/clinician/admission links. Its status uses 'NOT-GIVEN'->'GIVEN'; deletion is blocked when status=='GIVEN' ('Could not delete. Consumable already given to patient'). This tracks that a billable consumable item was given/charged, not a scheduled medication-dose administration event.
findings.cite: PatientConsumableChart.java:40-45 (qty/status/paymentType/membershipNo) and 67-70 (PatientBill OneToOne); PatientResource.java:3035-3036 (delete blocked when status GIVEN); PatientServiceImpl.java:2305 (chart.setStatus('NOT-GIVEN'))

findings.topic: PatientNursingChart does NOT track medication at all
findings.behaviour: PatientNursingChart columns are ONLY: feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar, drainageOutput, fluidIntake, urineOutput (all String) plus consultation/nonConsultation/admission/patient/nurse links and created* stamps. There are NO drug/dose-given columns. (Confirms the doc's hypothesis 'PatientNursingChart for dose-given columns' is FALSE.)
findings.cite: PatientNursingChart.java:38-45 (full column list; no medication fields)

findings.topic: Dose-given recency is derived from chart createdAt timestamps, not from a MAR event store
findings.behaviour: To show 'last given X ago', the code queries Prescriptions by patient+medicine+status 'GIVEN', then computes elapsed days/hours from a timestamp and renders 'Dosage : Last given N day(s)/hour(s) ago'. This is a derived UI string from issue/chart timestamps, not a queryable per-dose administration ledger.

findings.topic: Prescription-chart note is editable-window deletable (no immutability/audit)
findings.behaviour: deletePrescriptionChart hard-deletes a PatientPrescriptionChart if it is < 24 hours old (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(createdAt, now) >= 24 throws 'only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted'); otherwise it is physically deleted via repository.delete. There is no soft-delete, no version history, no Envers audit on these chart rows.

findings.topic: Angular frontend mirrors the backend exactly (no MAR UI)
findings.behaviour: IPatientPrescriptionChart interface exposes only id, dosage, output, remark plus the relation objects and created. The nurse inpatient chart component binds three plain strings (dosage/output/remark) and POSTs to /patients/save_patient_prescription_chart; it loads charts via /patients/prescription_charts_by_id. There is no administration-status, scheduling, due-list, or sign-off UI.

absences.expected: A dedicated MedicationAdministration / MAR entity recording discrete per-dose administration events (scheduled time, given time, given-by nurse, dose given, route, status given/held/refused/missed)
absences.searched: Grep (case-insensitive) MedicationAdministration|MAR|MedAdmin|administer|medication_administration|drugChart|DrugChart|medicationChart|MedicationChart across entire repo D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature (backend com.orbix.api + Angular zana-hmis) -> No files found. Glob domain/*Chart*.java lists only PatientConsumableChart, PatientDressingChart, PatientNursingChart, PatientObservationChart, PatientPrescriptionChart.

absences.expected: A 'pharmacy dispensedAt' timestamp distinct from administration
absences.searched: Grep dispens|dispensed across com.orbix.api -> No files found. Dispensing is represented only by Prescription/PharmacySaleOrderDetail status flipping NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN plus approvedAt/issuePharmacy in issueMedicine (PatientResource.java:3230-3237, 6259-6264). There is no separate dispensedAt field name.

absences.expected: A scheduled-dose / medication-administration-schedule (eMAR due list, missed-dose, held-dose) on inpatients
absences.searched: Inspected PatientPrescriptionChart.java (no schedule/status fields), Prescription.java (has dosage/frequency/route/days as free-text order strings at lines 42-45 but NO per-occurrence schedule rows), PatientNursingChart.java (no medication columns). No schedule entity found in domain/*.java glob.

absences.expected: A status / sign-off field on the medication chart (administered vs held vs refused)
absences.searched: PatientPrescriptionChart.java:34-82 has no status field; PatientPrescriptionChartModel.java:21-34 exposes none; Angular IPatientPrescriptionChart (patient-prescription-chart.ts:9-26) exposes none. Only dosage/output/remark free-text exist.

absences.expected: Hibernate Envers @Audited audit trail on medication-administration / chart entities
absences.searched: No @Audited anywhere on chart entities (consistent with known repo-wide phantom-feature finding); PatientPrescriptionChart.java, PatientConsumableChart.java, PatientNursingChart.java, PatientObservationChart.java carry no @Audited annotation. Chart edits are tracked only by createdBy/createdOn/createdAt and a 24h hard-delete window.

absences.expected: Device-fingerprint / device-binding around medication actions
absences.searched: Grep fingerprint|deviceId|device.binding|navigator.userAgent over repo -> only third-party vendor JS libraries (jquery-ui, summernote, ckeditor, dropzone, etc.) and package(-lock).json; no application device-binding logic. Confirms phantom feature; not present in the MAR/inpatient lane.

verdicts.planClaim: MedicationAdministration (MAR) is a MANDATORY aggregate (M15): a per-administered-dose entity with prescriptionLineUid, administeredAt, doseGiven, routeUid, administeredByUsername, patientResponse, linking back to Prescription in the pharmacy context by uid (doc lines 21, 108).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis exists. Whole-repo case-insensitive grep for MedicationAdministration|MedAdmin|drugChart|medicationChart|administeredAt|administeredBy over D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature returns 'No files found' (verified). Glob of domain/*Chart*.java lists ONLY PatientConsumableChart, PatientDressingChart, PatientNursingChart, PatientObservationChart, PatientPrescriptionChart — no MedicationAdministration class, repository, endpoint, or Angular interface (verified). The closest artefact, PatientPrescriptionChart.java:38-81, has ONLY dosage/output/remark (all unconstrained String), relation FKs, and createdBy/createdOn/createdAt — NO status, NO administered flag, NO scheduled/due time, NO route/frequency on the chart, and NO administeredAt distinct from createdAt. None of the six asserted fields (prescriptionLineUid, administeredAt, doseGiven, routeUid, administeredByUsername, patientResponse) exists on any legacy entity. This is a net-new closed-loop MAR feature, not a reproduction of legacy behaviour. A modern MAR is a legitimate clinical-safety improvement but under 'modern design, exact process' it must be parked as a NEW-FEATURE change request, not asserted as exact-process.
verdicts.correction: Re-label MAR as NEW SCOPE / change request (not exact-process reproduction). The doc should state explicitly: 'The legacy Zana HMIS has NO medication-administration record; inpatient medication tracking consists only of (1) Prescription order + String status pharmacy issue, (2) PatientPrescriptionChart free-text nurse dosing notes (dosage/output/remark), (3) PatientConsumableChart billed consumables. A discrete per-dose MAR aggregate is a net-new clinical-safety feature requiring an approved CR; engagement-lead approval at the phase gate is required before it enters the backlog as buildable.' Keep the aggregate design, but its acceptance criteria must say 'net-new requirement approved via CR', not imply it fixes a legacy gap.

verdicts.planClaim: M15 rationale: 'the prior build tracked only pharmacy dispensedAt' (doc line 88), framing MAR as a fix for a missing administration timestamp.
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has NO field named dispensedAt anywhere: grep dispens|dispensed across com.orbix.api returns 'No files found'. Dispensing is represented solely by Prescription.status flipping NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN in issueMedicine (PatientResource.java:3217-3237, verified: line 3230 setStatus('GIVEN'), line 3231 setIssuePharmacy, lines 3233-3235 approvedBy/On/At) plus PharmacySaleOrderDetail (PatientResource.java:6242-6264). The 'last given N ago' UI string is derived from Prescription.approvedAt (PatientResource.java:4496-4512, verified). So the doc invents a non-existent legacy field name (dispensedAt) and mischaracterizes the legacy state. The legacy did not 'track administration via dispensedAt'; it tracked pharmacy issue via a String status flip and an approvedAt stamp, and recorded free-text nurse dosing notes (PatientPrescriptionChart) with no administration semantics.
verdicts.correction: Replace 'the prior build tracked only pharmacy dispensedAt' with: 'Legacy has no dispensedAt field and no administration record. Pharmacy issue is a Prescription.status NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN flip plus an approvedAt stamp (PatientResource.java:3230-3235); nurse dosing is a free-text PatientPrescriptionChart note (dosage/output/remark) with no status, no scheduled dose, and no administeredAt distinct from createdAt. A MAR is therefore entirely net-new, not a replacement for a dispensedAt timestamp.'

verdicts.planClaim: MAR row 'links back to Prescription in pharmacy context via uid only (M15 fix)' and golden-master must 'administer a drug from the admission's active prescriptions and verify the MAR row is retrievable with the correct prescription line uid and administered-by username' (doc lines 21, 74).
verdicts.evidence: Two layers of invention. (a) The uid-only cross-module link and MAR retrieval-by-prescription-line-uid are net-new because the MAR entity itself is net-new (see first verdict). (b) Legacy has no 'prescription line uid': Prescription identity is a Long IDENTITY id (Prescription.java:40-41), there is no uid on Prescription, and there is no separate prescription-LINE concept — Prescription IS the line (one row per medicine). The legacy nurse-dosing write (PatientPrescriptionChart, PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590, verified) links to a Prescription by ManyToOne FK id, requires the Prescription.status == 'GIVEN' (line 2544), is INPATIENT-ONLY (rejects consultation/nonConsultation lines 2564-2569), and is a pure note write with NO stock/charge/Prescription mutation. So the 'administer a drug ... MAR row retrievable by prescription line uid + administered-by username' golden-master tests a net-new design, not legacy parity.
verdicts.correction: Acceptance criterion must be labelled net-new (CR-derived), not 'M15 fix' implying legacy parity. The uid-cross-module-reference design is a fine modern choice but is architectural, not behavioural reproduction. If legacy parity is also desired, add a separate criterion preserving the actual legacy constraints: a dosing note may only be written when the linked Prescription.status == 'GIVEN' (pharmacy-dispensed), inpatient-only, and only while admission.status == 'IN-PROCESS' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544-2577).

verdicts.planClaim: MAR uses a typed routeUid (route reference) and a structured patientResponse field per administered dose (doc line 21).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy route reference entity or patientResponse field is linked to administration. In legacy, route is a free-text String on the Prescription ORDER (Prescription.java:44 'private String route'), not a typed reference and not per-dose. There is no patientResponse concept anywhere in the medication lane — the nurse note offers only free-text dosage/output/remark (PatientPrescriptionChart.java:38-40, verified). Both routeUid (typed reference) and patientResponse are net-new modern fields with no legacy antecedent. This mirrors the inc-06 lesson (typed states/entities asserted over a system that used free-text Strings).
verdicts.correction: Flag routeUid and patientResponse as net-new structured fields under the MAR CR. If a legacy-derived hint is wanted, note that legacy route is free-text on the order (Prescription.route String) — promoting it to a typed reference is a modelling improvement, not exact-process, and must not be presented as legacy reproduction.

verdicts.planClaim: Doc line 100 (Definition-of-Done line, matched but body omitted) and line 108 assert the MAR aggregate as a hard DoD gate and line 116 lists 'MAR round-trip' as a behavioural golden-master parity test.
verdicts.evidence: It is internally consistent for the doc to gate delivery of the MAR aggregate it specifies and to round-trip test it. The drift is only in labelling: calling it a 'behavioural golden-master PARITY test' (line 116) is wrong because there is no legacy behaviour to be at parity WITH — confirmed by the absence of any MAR entity/store (grep + glob verified). A round-trip persistence test is valid; a 'parity' test is not, since the legacy baseline does not exist.
verdicts.correction: Keep the MAR creation/round-trip DoD items but reclassify the line-116 'MAR round-trip' from 'behavioural golden-master parity test' to 'net-new feature acceptance test (no legacy baseline)'. Genuine parity golden-masters in this increment (3-night accrual, second-approver gate, CASH discharge block, deceased guard) remain valid; MAR is not one of them.

verdicts.planClaim: Planning-doc hypothesis (implied in inc-06/07 lineage) that PatientNursingChart carries dose-given columns usable as a MAR source.
verdicts.evidence: PatientNursingChart.java:38-45 columns are ONLY feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar, drainageOutput, fluidIntake, urineOutput (all String) plus relation links and created* stamps — NO drug/dose columns whatsoever. Any expectation that an existing nursing chart supplies medication-administration data is contradicted by the legacy entity. (The doc itself does not directly source MAR from PatientNursingChart, so this is recorded as a legacy ground-truth correction guarding against that assumption rather than a quote from the doc.)
verdicts.correction: Confirm in the requirements that no existing legacy chart (Nursing, Observation, Dressing, Consumable, Prescription) carries medication-administration columns. The only medication-touching chart, PatientPrescriptionChart, is free-text dosing notes — explicitly NOT a dose-event source. MAR must be built fresh under the CR.

newDriftFound: DRIFT (field invention): doc line 88 asserts legacy 'tracked only pharmacy dispensedAt' — legacy has NO dispensedAt field; grep dispens|dispensed across com.orbix.api returns no files. Dispensing is a Prescription.status NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN flip + approvedAt stamp (PatientResource.java:3230-3235). The doc invents a non-existent legacy field name.

PHANTOM (prescription line uid): doc lines 21/74 reference a 'prescription LINE uid' in the pharmacy context. Legacy Prescription has a Long IDENTITY id and NO uid (Prescription.java:40-41); there is no separate prescription-line concept — Prescription is itself the per-medicine line. The uid and line abstraction are net-new modern design.

DRIFT (mislabelled parity test): doc line 116 lists 'MAR round-trip' among 'behavioural golden-master PARITY tests'. No legacy MAR behaviour exists to be at parity with; it can only be a net-new-feature acceptance test, not a parity test.

PHANTOM (typed route + patientResponse): MAR's routeUid (typed route reference) and patientResponse have no legacy antecedent; legacy route is free-text String on the Prescription order (Prescription.java:44) and there is no patientResponse field anywhere in the medication lane.

OBSERVATION (lost legacy constraints): the legacy nurse-dosing write enforces real preconditions the doc omits — linked Prescription.status must == 'GIVEN' (pharmacy-dispensed) else 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy'; inpatient-only (rejects outpatients/outsiders); admission.status must be IN-PROCESS (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544-2577). If the MAR CR is approved, these constraints should be carried forward as acceptance criteria so the new feature still respects the legacy ordering/dispense gate.

PHANTOM (no audit/immutability in legacy): the doc's DoD (line 114) implies auditable MAR events; legacy chart rows carry NO Envers @Audited and are hard-deletable within a 24h window (PatientResource.java:3109-3121). Tamper-evident MAR audit is net-new (consistent with the known repo-wide Envers-phantom finding), not a ported behaviour.

extract.lane: Consumable issue + stock decrement + charge accrual on inpatients
extract.summary: A "consumable" in the legacy system is NOT a stock-managed item. The `Consumable` entity (consumables table) is pure master-data: it is a OneToOne pointer to a `Medicine` plus audit columns, with NO quantity, batch, or balance field — it merely flags "this Medicine is a consumable." Issuing a consumable to an inpatient is done by POST /patients/save_patient_consumable_chart -> PatientServiceImpl.savePatientConsumableChart (line 2250), which creates a `PatientConsumableChart` row (qty as primitive double, free-text String status set to "NOT-GIVEN") and a `PatientBill` charge ("Medication"/"Consumable: <name>"). The charge IS accrued here (PatientBill + optional PatientInvoice/PatientInvoiceDetail). There is NO stock entity touched at all: `Medicine` has only a `price` (no qty), and no `PharmacyMedicineBatch`/`PrescriptionBatch`/`StoreItemBatch`/`StockCard`/stock-balance is read or decremented anywhere in the consumable path. There is no "give consumable" endpoint that would transition status to "GIVEN" and no negative-stock gate or locking. The inpatient consumable draws from NO stock — it is a billing-only event with zero inventory effect.

findings.topic: Consumable entity is master-data only (no stock field)
findings.behaviour: Consumable (table 'consumables') = @OneToOne Medicine (non-null, non-updatable) + createdBy/createdOn/createdAt only. NO qty, NO balance, NO batch, NO store/pharmacy link. It only marks a Medicine as a consumable.

findings.topic: Medicine has no stock/quantity field
findings.behaviour: Medicine entity has code, name, type, price (double), uom, active, category — but NO quantity/stock/balance field. So a consumable charge prices off Medicine.price; there is no inventory level on the master record.

findings.topic: PatientConsumableServiceImpl is empty (no logic)
findings.behaviour: The dedicated PatientConsumableServiceImpl class body is completely empty — no issue/stock/charge logic lives here. All issue logic is in PatientServiceImpl.

findings.topic: Issue entrypoint (controller)
findings.behaviour: POST /patients/save_patient_consumable_chart accepts a PatientConsumableChart body + consultation_id, non_consultation_id, admission_id, nurse_id params; resolves each by id and delegates to patientService.savePatientConsumableChart. No stock parameter, no batch parameter.

findings.topic: Issue service method — guards
findings.behaviour: savePatientConsumableChart: rejects if Medicine not found; rejects if qty <= 0 (InvalidEntryException 'Qty can not be zero'); rejects if the Medicine is not registered in consumables table (NotFoundException 'Medicine is not listed as consumable'); rejects if nurse missing; enforces exactly one of consultation/nonConsultation/admission. Explicitly throws for outpatients (consultation) and outsiders (nonConsultation) — consumable charting is admission-only in practice.

findings.topic: Admission-status gate (HARD reject)
findings.behaviour: For admission path: if Admission.status == 'PENDING' -> reject 'Admission not verified'; if 'IN-PROCESS' -> proceed; any other status -> reject 'Patient already signed off'. Admission.status is a free-text String compared by .equals.

findings.topic: Chart status is free-text String, initialized NOT-GIVEN
findings.behaviour: PatientConsumableChart.status is a plain String (not an enum). On save it is hard-coded to 'NOT-GIVEN'. qty is primitive double. paymentType/membershipNo are free-text Strings. Links: admission, medicine, patientBill (OneToOne, non-null), patient, nurse, optional clinician/insurancePlan.

findings.topic: Charge accrual — PatientBill created
findings.behaviour: A PatientBill is always created: amount=Medicine.price, paid=0, balance=price*qty, qty=chart.qty, billItem='Medication', description='Consumable: <name>', status='UNPAID', patient set. Saved before insurance/admission branch.

findings.topic: Charge accrual — insurance-covered branch
findings.behaviour: If patient.paymentType=='INSURANCE' OR admission present: looks up MedicineInsurancePlan (covered=true). If covered: amount=paid=planPrice*qty, balance=0, status='COVERED', paymentType='INSURANCE', and adds a PatientInvoiceDetail to a PENDING PatientInvoice (creating the invoice with no='id' if none exists). Note invoice detail qty hard-coded to 1.

findings.topic: Charge accrual — admission non-covered (cash-on-admission) branch
findings.behaviour: If no covered MedicineInsurancePlan but admission present: amount=balance=price*qty, paid=0, status='VERIFIED' (i.e. auto-verified/charged to admission account, not requiring upfront payment), attached to a PENDING insurancePlan=null PatientInvoice via PatientInvoiceDetail (qty hard-coded 1).

findings.topic: Chart persisted last; links patient + patientBill
findings.behaviour: After bill/invoice work: chart.patient and chart.patientBill set, audit fields set, then patientConsumableChartRepository.save(chart). This is the only persistence of the chart. No stock write precedes or follows it.

findings.topic: Delete path references GIVEN guard + credit note
findings.behaviour: POST /patients/delete_consumable_chart: blocks delete if status=='GIVEN' ('Consumable already given to patient'); blocks if record older than 24h; if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists, raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote (reference text mislabeled 'Canceled lab test'); deletes PatientInvoiceDetail (and empty PatientInvoice), payment detail, the chart, and the PatientBill. No stock is restored because none was decremented.

findings.topic: ConsumableResource is pure master-data CRUD
findings.behaviour: ConsumableResource exposes list/get/get_by_procedure_type/load_like (open reads) and add/delete guarded by @PreAuthorize ADMIN-ACCESS. add() requires the Medicine to exist and to not already be a consumable. No issue/stock/charge endpoints here.

findings.topic: Contrast: prescription lane DOES touch stock; consumable lane does NOT
findings.behaviour: The only setStatus('GIVEN') transitions in PatientServiceImpl are for Prescription (line 3230) and a prescription detail (line 6259), which are the medicine-dispensing flows. PatientConsumableChart has no 'GIVEN' transition method anywhere — its only 'GIVEN' reference is the read-only delete guard at PatientResource.java:3035.

absences.expected: A ConsumableStockBalance entity / dedicated consumable stock ledger
absences.searched: Glob domain/*Stock*.java and *Batch*.java (found only PharmacyStockCard, StoreStockCard, PharmacyMedicineBatch, PrescriptionBatch, StoreItemBatch, StoreToPharmacyBatch, PharmacyToPharmacyBatch, GoodsReceivedNoteDetailBatch, PharmacyToPharmacyBatch); none is consumable-specific or referenced in the consumable path. No ConsumableStockBalance class exists.

absences.expected: Stock decrement when a consumable is issued to an inpatient
absences.searched: Read savePatientConsumableChart (PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475) in full; grep'd PharmacyMedicineBatch|StoreItemBatch|PrescriptionBatch|StockCard in PatientServiceImpl -> 'No matches found'; Medicine has no qty field (Medicine.java:38-62); Consumable has no qty field (Consumable.java:35-50). NO stock of any kind (Medicine.qty, store stock, pharmacy stock, batch) is read or decremented. The inpatient consumable draws from NO stock entity.

absences.expected: A 'give consumable' / status->GIVEN transition that would decrement stock at point of administration
absences.searched: Grep GIVEN across PatientResource.java and PatientServiceImpl.java; PatientService.java interface (only declares savePatientConsumableChart, line 68). No update/give endpoint or method exists for PatientConsumableChart; status is set to NOT-GIVEN at creation and never advanced anywhere in code. 'GIVEN' for consumables appears only as a delete-guard read (PatientResource.java:3035).

absences.expected: Negative-stock prevention gate / FIFO-FEFO batch consumption for consumables
absences.searched: savePatientConsumableChart guards (PatientServiceImpl.java:2252-2303) only check Medicine existence, qty>0, consumable registration, nurse, and admission status. NO stock-availability check, NO batch selection, NO FEFO/FIFO ordering. Absent entirely.

absences.expected: Locking (pessimistic/@Lock/synchronized/SELECT FOR UPDATE) around the issue
absences.searched: Grep Lock|Pessimistic|synchronized|select for update in PatientServiceImpl returned no matches in the consumable path; method relies only on the class-level javax.transaction.@Transactional (PatientServiceImpl class annotation). No row/stock locking exists because no stock row is touched.

absences.expected: PatientConsumableServiceImpl business logic
absences.searched: Read service/PatientConsumableServiceImpl.java in full — class body is empty (lines 25-27). All consumable issue logic lives in PatientServiceImpl, not this service.

verdicts.planClaim: ConsumableIssue line decrements ConsumableStockBalance PESSIMISTICALLY (ADR-0017 @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)) in the same transaction as the chart entry (doc lines 24, 94, 109).
verdicts.evidence: GROUND TRUTH: the legacy consumable-issue path touches NO stock entity at all. Consumable (domain/Consumable.java:35-50) is a @OneToOne pointer to Medicine with only audit cols — no qty/batch/balance. Medicine (domain/Medicine.java:38-62) has price but NO quantity/stock field. savePatientConsumableChart (service/PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475) was read in full and grep of PharmacyMedicineBatch|StoreItemBatch|PrescriptionBatch|StockCard in PatientServiceImpl returned NO matches in the consumable path. No stock row is read or decremented, so there is nothing to lock. CONFIRMED IN-REPO: grep for ConsumableStockBalance/decrementForIssue across HMSCLEAN2 finds ZERO Java source — only planning/discovery .md/.mjs files. Same class as the inc-08 D6 finding (08 RECONCILIATION line 49 'NO StockBalance entity'; line 81 'pessimistic lock is net-new hardening, not reproduction'). The entity, the decrement, and the pessimistic lock all have no legacy basis.
verdicts.correction: Remove ConsumableStockBalance, the pessimistic decrement, and ADR-0017 from the consumable lane. Legacy consumable issue is a billing-only event with ZERO inventory effect. Model ConsumableIssue/PatientConsumableChart as: resolve Medicine, guard qty>0, guard Medicine is registered as a consumable, guard nurse present, guard admission status, then create PatientBill (+optional invoice detail) and persist the chart with status 'NOT-GIVEN'. If a consumable stock ledger is genuinely desired, park it as a NET-NEW change request (CR), not a legacy-reproduction acceptance criterion.

verdicts.planClaim: Dependency: 'inc-08 ConsumableStockBalance and ConsumableStockService.decrementForIssue must exist before the consumable chart can issue stock atomically (overdraft refusal)' (doc line 60), and inc-08 is built before inc-07 specifically for this.
verdicts.evidence: Double phantom. (1) inc-08 as actually built created PharmacyMedicine.stock (plain double, read-modify-write) + PharmacyMedicineBatch + StockService — there is NO ConsumableStockBalance and NO decrementForIssue (08 RECONCILIATION line 49 'NO StockBalance entity'; in-repo grep finds these names only in planning docs, zero Java). (2) Even if such a service existed, the legacy consumable path would NOT call it: the consumable issue draws from no stock (PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475; Medicine.java:38-62; Consumable.java:35-50). The inc-08 doc's own forward reference (08 RECONCILIATION line 185 'consumes the inc-08 stock ledger via ConsumableStockService.decrementForIssue') is itself unverified and propagates the same phantom across both increments.
verdicts.correction: Delete this dependency. inc-07 consumable issue has NO inc-08 stock dependency — it depends only on Medicine/Consumable master data (for pricing + the consumable-registration guard) and on billing.api.recordClinicalCharge for the PatientBill. The 'overdraft refusal' premise is invalid because no stock is consumed. Reassess whether inc-08-before-inc-07 ordering is still justified on this basis (it is not, for consumables).

verdicts.planClaim: Consumable charge accrued synchronously via billing.api.recordClinicalCharge in the same transaction as the issue (doc lines 24, 58, 95).
verdicts.evidence: The charge-accrual-in-tx behaviour IS legacy-faithful. savePatientConsumableChart always creates a PatientBill synchronously (amount=Medicine.price, balance=price*qty, billItem='Medication', description='Consumable: <name>', status='UNPAID') BEFORE the insurance/admission branch (PatientServiceImpl.java:2307-2319), under the class-level @Transactional, in the same unit of work as the chart persist (chart saved last at 2467-2474). Synchronous in-tx accrual (ADR-0008 rule 4) is the right re-model. CORRECTIONS: the legacy logic is richer than a single recordClinicalCharge call — (a) INSURANCE/admission path looks up MedicineInsurancePlan(covered=true): if covered, amount=paid=planPrice*qty, balance=0, status='COVERED', and appends a PatientInvoiceDetail to a PENDING PatientInvoice (2321-2395); (b) admission non-covered (cash-on-admission) path: amount=balance=price*qty, paid=0, status='VERIFIED', attached to a PENDING null-plan invoice (2396-2465); (c) invoice-detail qty is hard-coded to 1 in BOTH branches (quirk to preserve). PatientBill is OneToOne non-null on the chart.
verdicts.correction: Keep synchronous in-tx accrual, but the acceptance criteria must reproduce the three-way bill outcome (UNPAID cash default; COVERED insured via MedicineInsurancePlan with PatientInvoiceDetail; VERIFIED cash-on-admission) and the hard-coded invoiceDetail qty=1, not a single generic recordClinicalCharge. Confirm with legacy-analyst whether billing.api.recordClinicalCharge can express all three statuses/invoice attachments.

verdicts.planClaim: ConsumableIssue line fields: consumableUid, qty NUMERIC(19,6), unitCost (snapshot Money), sourceKind ∈ {PHARMACY, STORE}, sourceLocationUid (doc line 24).
verdicts.evidence: Partially invented. Legacy PatientConsumableChart (domain/PatientConsumableChart.java:40-96) holds: admission, medicine, patientBill (OneToOne non-null), patient, nurse, optional clinician/insurancePlan, qty (primitive double), status (free-text String 'NOT-GIVEN'), paymentType/membershipNo (free-text Strings). There is NO sourceKind, NO sourceLocationUid, NO unitCost snapshot field on the chart — and no PHARMACY/STORE source concept anywhere in the consumable path because no stock location is involved. The price is read live from Medicine.price (or MedicineInsurancePlan), not snapshotted as a per-line unitCost on the chart.
verdicts.correction: sourceKind {PHARMACY,STORE} and sourceLocationUid are NET-NEW (CR) — there is no legacy source-location dispensing for consumables. unitCost-as-line-snapshot is a reasonable modern hardening but is net-new vs legacy live Medicine.price read; flag it. qty re-modelled double->NUMERIC(19,6) is pre-approved data-type modernization (ACCEPTABLE). Drop the stock-source fields or move them to a CR; preserve the actual links (admission/medicine/nurse/patientBill/optional clinician/insurancePlan, paymentType, membershipNo, free-text status).

verdicts.planClaim: Exact-process target #5 / DoD: concurrent issue of the LAST UNIT of a consumable must result in one success and one 409 STOCK_INSUFFICIENT (Testcontainers two-parallel-request concurrency test) (doc lines 72, 118).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis. The savePatientConsumableChart guards (PatientServiceImpl.java:2252-2303) check ONLY: Medicine exists, qty>0 ('Qty can not be zero'), Medicine registered as consumable ('Medicine is not listed as consumable'), nurse present, exactly-one-of consultation/nonConsultation/admission, and admission status (PENDING->reject 'Admission not verified'; IN-PROCESS->proceed; else->'Patient already signed off'). There is NO stock-availability check, NO 'last unit', NO STOCK_INSUFFICIENT error, NO batch selection, NO FEFO/FIFO, and NO locking (grep Lock|Pessimistic|synchronized|select for update returned no matches in the consumable path). A 'last unit' cannot exist because no stock balance exists.
verdicts.correction: Remove this fidelity target and DoD line for consumables. There is no concurrency/overdraft semantics to reproduce. If overdraft refusal on consumables is wanted, it is NET-NEW and belongs in a CR with acceptance criteria explicitly labelled net-new — not as exact-process parity.

verdicts.planClaim: POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{uid}/consumables — 'consumable issue (decrements stock + accrues charge)' (doc lines 46-47).
verdicts.evidence: The endpoint+chart concept maps to legacy POST /patients/save_patient_consumable_chart -> patientService.savePatientConsumableChart (api/PatientResource.java:2152-2167), and the GET consumable chart is reasonable. The annotation '(decrements stock + accrues charge)' is HALF-WRONG: accrues charge = TRUE (PatientBill, 2307-2319); decrements stock = FALSE (no stock entity touched). NOTE the legacy endpoint also accepts consultation_id/non_consultation_id/admission_id/nurse_id and enforces exactly-one; for inpatient it is admission-only in practice (outpatient/outsider paths explicitly throw).
verdicts.correction: Reword endpoint description to 'consumable issue (creates PatientConsumableChart + accrues PatientBill charge; no stock effect)'. Keep admission-scoped path but ensure the service reproduces the nurse_id requirement and the admission-status gate (PENDING reject / IN-PROCESS proceed / else 'Patient already signed off').

verdicts.planClaim: PatientConsumableChart status is a typed lifecycle that advances to GIVEN at administration (implied by 'consumable stock integration' and stock-decrement-at-issue framing; doc line 5, lines 24/46).
verdicts.evidence: status is a plain free-text String hard-coded to 'NOT-GIVEN' on save and NEVER advanced anywhere in code (domain/PatientConsumableChart.java:40-96; set at PatientServiceImpl.java:2305). There is NO 'give consumable' endpoint and NO setStatus('GIVEN') for consumables — the only GIVEN transitions in PatientServiceImpl are for Prescription (3230) and a prescription detail (6259). The single 'GIVEN' reference for consumables is a READ-ONLY delete guard ('Consumable already given to patient', PatientResource.java:3035). PatientConsumableServiceImpl is an empty class (service/PatientConsumableServiceImpl.java:25-27). Modelling a typed advancing status (like the inc-06 typed-state phantoms) over a never-transitioned free-text field is the same drift class.
verdicts.correction: Model status as the legacy free-text/string set to 'NOT-GIVEN' at creation with NO automatic GIVEN transition and NO administration-time stock decrement. The only place 'GIVEN' matters is the delete guard. If a GIVEN administration transition is desired, it is NET-NEW (CR). Note legacy delete rules to preserve: block delete if status=='GIVEN', block if record >24h old, raise PENDING PatientCreditNote if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists (mislabeled 'Canceled lab test' ref), and cascade-delete invoice detail/payment/chart/bill (PatientResource.java:3026-3085) — with NO stock restoration because none was decremented.

newDriftFound: INVOICE-DETAIL QTY QUIRK (not in plan): the legacy hard-codes PatientInvoiceDetail.qty = 1 in BOTH the insurance-covered branch (PatientServiceImpl.java:2321-2395) and the cash-on-admission branch (2396-2465), even though the bill qty = chart.qty. A faithful build must reproduce this qty=1 invoice-detail quirk; the plan's generic recordClinicalCharge framing would silently lose it. Confirm with legacy-analyst whether this is intentional or a latent legacy bug before authoring AC.

BILL STATUS TRICHOTOMY (not in plan): consumable PatientBill status is one of UNPAID (cash default, 2307-2319), COVERED (insured w/ MedicineInsurancePlan covered=true, 2321-2395), or VERIFIED (cash-on-admission auto-charged to admission account, 2396-2465). The plan's single 'recordClinicalCharge' abstraction hides this three-way outcome and must be expanded into explicit acceptance criteria.

CONSUMABLE-REGISTRATION GUARD (not in plan): issue rejects with 'Medicine is not listed as consumable' if the Medicine is not present in the consumables table (PatientServiceImpl.java:2252-2289). This master-data prerequisite (Consumable row must exist for the Medicine) is a real legacy guard absent from the plan's AC and should be an explicit validation criterion.

ADMISSION-STATUS GATE STRINGS (not in plan): the issue gate uses free-text Admission.status .equals comparisons — 'PENDING' -> reject 'Admission not verified', 'IN-PROCESS' -> proceed, any other -> reject 'Patient already signed off' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2290-2303). The plan models a typed admission state machine (ADMITTED->...); the consumable gate's exact legacy strings/messages must be preserved or the status mapping explicitly documented as a re-model (legacy 'IN-PROCESS' is the admitted/active state, NOT the plan's 'ADMITTED' label).

DELETE/CREDIT-NOTE PATH MISSING FROM SCOPE: the plan lists POST/GET consumable endpoints but omits the legacy delete_consumable_chart flow (PatientResource.java:3026-3085) with its GIVEN guard, 24h window, PatientCreditNote-on-paid, and cascade delete. Either descope explicitly or add a story; the credit-note reference text is mislabeled 'Canceled lab test' in legacy (preserve or fix-via-CR).

extract.lane: Ward charges / ward-day billing + the bills-cleared discharge gate
extract.summary: Ward charging is NOT a single flat charge and NOT a Spring @Scheduled job. At admission (PatientServiceImpl.doAdmission, line 1701) the legacy creates exactly ONE ward-bed PatientBill (qty=1, billItem="Bed", description="Ward Bed / Room") priced from WardType.price (a single flat double; there is no per-day rate field). Per-day accrual is performed by a hand-rolled background thread: UpdatePatient implements Runnable with a while(true){ sleep(300000) } 5-minute poll loop, instantiated and started via a plain new Thread(...).start() inside a @Bean void method (MainApplication.updateRecords, line 160-184). On each pass it scans admissions with status IN-PROCESS or STOPPED; for any AdmissionBed in status OPENED whose openedAt is >= 24 HOURS old (ChronoUnit.HOURS, UpdatePatient line 291-292), it sets that AdmissionBed to CLOSED/closedAt, then creates a NEW ward-bed PatientBill (status VERIFIED) and a NEW OPENED AdmissionBed dated now (line 294-334). So accrual is event-poll driven, not cron/ShedLock; if the thread is not running, no ward-days accrue, and accrual stops once the admission leaves IN-PROCESS/STOPPED. The price for INSURANCE patients is resolved from WardTypeInsurancePlan (findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan,true)) by a max-price/plan-match loop, with a supplementary "Top up" PatientBill for the WardType.price minus covered price difference. The bills-cleared discharge gate is a HARD service reject (throws InvalidOperationException), implemented identically in three endpoints (get_discharge_summary line 5346-5349, get_referral_summary line 5597-5601, get_deceased_summary line 5856-5859) by iterating PatientInvoice->PatientInvoiceDetail->PatientBill.getStatus() and rejecting if ANY equals "UNPAID" or "VERIFIED"; it is NOT a worklist filter. Admission.status, AdmissionBed.status and DischargePlan.status are all free-text Strings, not typed enums, and DischargePlan has no typed "kind".

findings.topic: Ward charge at admission is a single one-off PatientBill, qty=1, not multi-day
findings.behaviour: doAdmission creates one PatientBill with amount=WardType.price, qty(1), billItem="Bed", description="Ward Bed / Room", status="UNPAID", plus an AdmissionBed (status OPENED, openedAt=now) linking the bill. No loop, no day count.

findings.topic: Ward price source = WardType.price (flat double, no per-day rate field)
findings.behaviour: Cash ward price is read as wb.getWard().getWardType().getPrice(); WardType entity has a single 'private double price = 0' and NO daily-rate or per-night field.

findings.topic: NO Spring @Scheduled / cron / ShedLock anywhere
findings.behaviour: Grep for @Scheduled, @EnableScheduling, cron, ShedLock, @Async across the entire ZANAHMIS-2-feature project returned NO matches. There is no Spring-managed scheduler for ward-day accrual.

findings.topic: Per-day ward accrual IS implemented, but as a manual background polling Thread (Runnable), not a cron job
findings.behaviour: UpdatePatient implements Runnable; run() is an infinite while(true) loop that Thread.sleep(300000) (5 min) each pass and re-scans admissions. It is the only ward-day accrual mechanism.

findings.topic: How the accrual thread is started
findings.behaviour: MainApplication has a @Bean-annotated void method updateRecords() that does 'new UpdatePatient(...)' then 'new Thread(updatePatient).start()'. Started once at context init via the bean method; not @PostConstruct, not CommandLineRunner, not @Scheduled.

findings.topic: Ward-day accrual trigger condition = AdmissionBed OPENED for >= 24 hours
findings.behaviour: For each admission in status IN-PROCESS or STOPPED, it finds the OPENED AdmissionBed and computes ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now); if difference >= 24 it closes the old AdmissionBed (CLOSED + closedAt) and opens a new one + new bill.

findings.topic: Accrued (subsequent-day) ward bill is created with status VERIFIED, not UNPAID
findings.behaviour: The new ward-day PatientBill in the accrual path is created with setStatus("VERIFIED") (the admission-time bill at doAdmission uses "UNPAID"). Both later become "COVERED" if insurance-eligible.

findings.topic: Accrual only runs for IN-PROCESS or STOPPED admissions
findings.behaviour: admissionStatuses list = {"IN-PROCESS","STOPPED"}; findAllByStatusIn(...) drives the loop. PENDING admissions and SIGNED-OUT/HELD admissions do not accrue ward-days.

findings.topic: Duplicate-OPENED-bed cleanup branch
findings.behaviour: If an admission has more than one OPENED AdmissionBed (no>1), it closes them (CLOSED+closedAt) and 'continue's WITHOUT creating a new charge that pass.

findings.topic: Insurance ward price resolution via WardTypeInsurancePlan
findings.behaviour: For INSURANCE patients, doAdmission queries wardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) and loops choosing the highest price OR an exact plan-id match (breaks on exact match). Eligible plan sets bill amount/paid, balance 0, status COVERED.

findings.topic: WardTypeInsurancePlan eligibility query ignores the 'active' flag
findings.behaviour: Selection uses findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) only; WardTypeInsurancePlan.active is never part of the query or the selection loop, so an inactive-but-covered plan is still eligible.

findings.topic: Supplementary 'Top up' ward bill for non-exact insurance plans
findings.behaviour: If eligiblePlan is a different plan than the patient's own AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0, a second PatientBill is created amount=difference, status UNPAID, description "Ward Bed / Room (Top up)", linked as supplementary/principal.

findings.topic: Bills-cleared discharge gate is a HARD service reject (throw), not a filter
findings.behaviour: get_discharge_summary iterates patientInvoiceRepository.findAllByAdmission -> PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill.getStatus(); if any equals "UNPAID" OR "VERIFIED" it throws InvalidOperationException("Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills."). Only after passing does it approve invoices and sign out.

findings.topic: Same hard gate duplicated in referral discharge path
findings.behaviour: get_referral_summary applies the identical UNPAID/VERIFIED reject over findAllByAdmission invoice details for the admission branch.

findings.topic: Same hard gate duplicated in deceased discharge path
findings.behaviour: get_deceased_summary applies the identical UNPAID/VERIFIED reject over both admission invoices and consultation invoices before signing out.

findings.topic: Discharge status transition: STOPPED -> SIGNED-OUT on bill clearance
findings.behaviour: After the gate passes, if admission.status=="STOPPED" it sets SIGNED-OUT, stamps dischargedBy/On/At from the DischargePlan, sets WardBed status EMPTY, and resets patient to OUTPATIENT/CASH/insurancePlan=null.

findings.topic: Admission becomes STOPPED only when a discharge or referral plan is first saved
findings.behaviour: saveDischargePlan sets admission.setStatus("STOPPED") (line 5269); saveReferralPlan sets admission STOPPED (line 5451). Deceased path uses HELD instead (line 5725).

findings.topic: Admission.status is free-text String, not a typed enum
findings.behaviour: Admission.status is 'private String status' with observed values PENDING (set at doAdmission 1719), IN-PROCESS (1959 / PatientResource 5721), STOPPED, HELD, SIGNED-OUT — all string literals, no enum type.

findings.topic: Admission has only flat dischargedBy/On/At fields; no typed discharge classification
findings.behaviour: Admission entity carries dischargedBy(Long)/dischargedOn(Long)/dischargedAt(LocalDateTime) only; the disposition (discharge vs referral vs deceased) is encoded only by which plan entity exists, not by a field on Admission.

findings.topic: WardBed status is free-text String with values EMPTY/WAITING/OCCUPIED
findings.behaviour: WardBed.status String transitions: EMPTY->WAITING at admission start (1710), ->OCCUPIED when admission goes IN-PROCESS (PatientServiceImpl 1961-1962), ->EMPTY on sign-out. WardBed.active is a separate boolean gate checked at admission.

findings.topic: DischargePlan has no typed 'kind'; status is free-text String defaulting PENDING
findings.behaviour: DischargePlan fields are free-text clinical strings (history, investigation, management, operationNote, icuAdmissionNote, generalRecommendation) plus 'private String status = "PENDING"'. No kind/type/category enum.

absences.expected: A Spring @Scheduled / cron / ShedLock job for daily ward-day accrual
absences.searched: Grep @Scheduled|@EnableScheduling|cron|ShedLock|shedlock|@Async over entire D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature tree — NO matches. Accrual is instead a manual new Thread(new UpdatePatient()).start() polling loop (MainApplication.java:160-184, UpdatePatient.java:104-112).

absences.expected: A per-day / per-night ward rate field on WardType or WardTypeInsurancePlan
absences.searched: Read domain/WardType.java (only single 'double price') and domain/WardTypeInsurancePlan.java (only single 'double price') — NO dailyRate/perNight/ratePerDay field exists. Ward-day cost is just WardType.price re-charged per 24h interval.

absences.expected: A bills-cleared FILTER on the discharge worklist (loadDischargeList)
absences.searched: Read api/PatientResource.java loadDischargeList (5312-5327): it filters DischargePlan only by status IN {PENDING,APPROVED}; it does NOT filter on outstanding bills. The bills-cleared check exists ONLY as a hard reject inside get_discharge_summary / get_referral_summary / get_deceased_summary.

absences.expected: AdmissionBed being closed at discharge (status CLOSED / closedAt set on sign-out)
absences.searched: Grep AdmissionBed/setStatus("CLOSED")/setClosedAt in PatientResource.java and PatientServiceImpl.java — the discharge paths only set WardBed status EMPTY; they NEVER close the AdmissionBed. AdmissionBed is only ever closed inside UpdatePatient.java (accrual rollover at lines 276-278, 297-298). So the final OPENED AdmissionBed of a discharged admission is left OPENED.

absences.expected: A typed DischargePlan.kind / discharge-disposition enum
absences.searched: Read domain/DischargePlan.java — no kind/type field; disposition is implied only by which entity (DischargePlan vs ReferralPlan vs DeceasedNote) was created.

absences.expected: A separate MAR / WardTransfer / FluidBalance entity in the ward-billing path
absences.searched: Not referenced anywhere in doAdmission (PatientServiceImpl.java:1701-2021), the accrual loop (UpdatePatient.java:258-420), or the discharge gates (PatientResource.java:5329-5910). Out of lane scope but confirmed absent from all ward-charge/discharge code examined.

verdicts.planClaim: WardDayAccrualJob (JOB-001) is a Spring @Scheduled + ShedLock daily cron (`0 5 0 * * *`) producing one WARD InvoiceLine per night (line 25, 110, ADR-0018 JOB-001).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has NO @Scheduled/@EnableScheduling/cron/ShedLock/@Async anywhere (grep over D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature => no matches). Ward-day accrual IS implemented in legacy but as a hand-rolled `new Thread(new UpdatePatient()).start()` infinite while(true){ sleep(300000) } 5-min polling loop started from MainApplication.updateRecords (MainApplication.java:160-184; UpdatePatient.java:72,104-112). So the *mechanism* (Spring @Scheduled + ShedLock cron) is entirely net-new. NOTABLY, ADR-0018 itself (lines 19, 57, 111, 130-132) is honest that this is a 'deliberate process improvement, not a divergence' adopted to fix prior-attempt M23 — it is NOT a reproduction of legacy. The job is legitimate NET-NEW design that should be parked as an approved CR, but the planning doc's exact-process framing must not present it as legacy fidelity.
verdicts.correction: Keep the @Scheduled+ShedLock job as a ratified NET-NEW design (already justified in ADR-0018), but it must be explicitly tagged NEW SCOPE / process improvement in the increment doc and change-request register, not implied to reproduce legacy. The legacy mechanism is a 5-minute polling Thread (UpdatePatient) that re-charges per 24h AdmissionBed interval — not a midnight cron and not one-line-per-calendar-night.

verdicts.planClaim: Ward-day accrual is idempotent via a unique constraint on (admission_uid, business_date) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, producing exactly one WARD line per calendar night (line 25, 66, 110; exact-process target #2).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy accrual is NOT keyed on calendar business_date and is NOT one-line-per-calendar-night. It rolls over per AdmissionBed when ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now) >= 24 (UpdatePatient.java:258-299), i.e. a rolling 24h-from-openedAt interval, not a midnight calendar boundary. There is no (admission, business_date) uniqueness in legacy; idempotency there is implicit in the OPENED-bed age check. A constraint keyed on calendar date can produce a DIFFERENT night count than legacy for admissions that cross midnight (e.g., admit at 23:00 => legacy charges next bed at 23:00+24h; calendar-date model could charge at the 00:05 cron the very next morning, ~1h later). The 'exactly 3 lines for a 3-night stay totalling 3 x wardType.price' assertion is the NEW model's intent, not legacy arithmetic.
verdicts.correction: State that this idempotency key and per-calendar-night granularity is a NET-NEW accrual model (CR-tracked) that APPROXIMATES but does not bit-match the legacy rolling-24h-from-openedAt Thread behaviour. If true parity is required, the golden-master must reconcile against the legacy 24h-rolling rollover, not a calendar-date cron; otherwise document the known timing variance for admissions crossing midnight as an accepted deviation.

verdicts.planClaim: Ward-day price is resolved via ServicePrice(kind=WARD, serviceUid=wardTypeUid, planUid=insurancePlanUid|null) per ADR-0009 (line 25, 57, 66, 111).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy has no unified ServicePrice(kind=WARD) table. Cash ward price = wb.getWard().getWardType().getPrice() — a single flat `double price` on WardType with no per-day/per-night field (PatientServiceImpl.java:1754-1756; WardType.java:39-40). Insurance ward price = WardTypeInsurancePlan.findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) resolved by a max-price/exact-plan-match loop (PatientServiceImpl.java:1795-1819). ADR-0009 contains NO 'WARD' / 'ServicePrice' / 'kind' references (grep returned no matches). So 'ServicePrice(kind=WARD)' is a doc-invented pricing abstraction not grounded in legacy OR in the cited ADR.
verdicts.correction: Re-model to preserve the two distinct legacy price sources: WardType.price for CASH (ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS if re-homed in a service-price table that faithfully maps WardType->price) and WardTypeInsurancePlan (covered=true) for INSURANCE via the max-price-or-exact-plan-match selection loop. Drop the unsupported 'ServicePrice(kind=WARD)' phrasing unless that table is independently ratified and explicitly defined as the new home for both legacy sources.

verdicts.planClaim: Insurance ward pricing is just ServicePrice(kind=WARD, planUid).amount with a cash fallback (line 66, 111).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy insurance ward pricing is materially more complex and is NOT a simple plan-keyed lookup with cash fallback. (1) Selection loops over WardTypeInsurancePlan rows choosing the HIGHEST covered price OR breaking on an exact plan-id match (PatientServiceImpl.java:1799-1809). (2) The eligibility query findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan,true) IGNORES the WardTypeInsurancePlan.active flag — an inactive-but-covered plan is still eligible (WardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.java:40; WardTypeInsurancePlan.java:43-44). (3) If the eligible plan differs from the patient's own plan AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0, a SUPPLEMENTARY 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' PatientBill (status UNPAID) is created for the difference (PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1897). None of this top-up / max-price-loop / active-flag-ignored logic appears in the doc.
verdicts.correction: Author explicit stories/AC for: (a) the max-price-or-exact-plan-match selection loop, (b) the 'Top up' supplementary bill = WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price when >0 for non-exact plans, and (c) the documented legacy quirk that the active flag is ignored in eligibility (flag to legacy-analyst whether to preserve as-is or CR-fix). A simple plan-keyed lookup with cash fallback under-specifies legacy and will not reconcile.

verdicts.planClaim: The legacy charges one flat amount at admission; the fresh-build accrual model must produce the same total by discharge (line 5, 66, exact-process target #2).
verdicts.evidence: PARTIALLY true and partially false, hence net DRIFT as stated. TRUE: at admission doAdmission creates exactly ONE ward-bed PatientBill (qty=1, billItem='Bed', desc='Ward Bed / Room', status UNPAID) priced from WardType.price (PatientServiceImpl.java:1753-1783). FALSE: legacy does NOT stop there — the UpdatePatient polling Thread DOES re-accrue additional ward-bed PatientBills every 24h while the admission is IN-PROCESS or STOPPED (UpdatePatient.java:258-334). So legacy is NOT 'one flat charge'; it is 'one charge at admission (UNPAID) plus N additional 24h-interval charges (status VERIFIED) for the duration the thread runs'. The doc (and ADR-0018 line 15) both mis-state legacy as single-upfront-only, which understates the legacy total and mis-frames the parity target.
verdicts.correction: Correct the legacy characterization: legacy already accrues per-24h via the UpdatePatient Thread (first bill UNPAID at admission, subsequent bills VERIFIED). The new model therefore reproduces an EXISTING accrual behaviour with a different mechanism (cron vs polling thread) and different status semantics — not a single flat charge. Update ADR-0018 context line 15 and exact-process target #2 accordingly; the parity total is (1 + N) x WardType.price where N = completed 24h intervals, with timing dependent on whether the thread/job was running.

verdicts.planClaim: Accrued ward-day InvoiceLines are uniform (each amount = WardType.price); the doc models all ward lines identically (line 25, 66).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy ward bills are NOT uniform in status. The admission-time bill is status UNPAID (PatientServiceImpl.java:1760); every subsequent accrual-thread bill is status VERIFIED (UpdatePatient.java:304-311). Insurance-eligible bills become COVERED. The bills-cleared discharge gate keys on BOTH 'UNPAID' and 'VERIFIED' as uncleared — so the status distinction is load-bearing for discharge, not cosmetic.
verdicts.correction: Model ward-day lines with their legacy status semantics: first/admission line UNPAID, accrued lines VERIFIED, insurance lines COVERED; and ensure the discharge gate treats BOTH UNPAID and VERIFIED as outstanding (see bills-cleared gate verdict).

verdicts.planClaim: CASH discharge is blocked when admission.billsCleared = false; the billing dispatcher sets a denormalised bills_cleared flag when the invoice balance reaches zero (line 13, 52, 70, 93, 106; exact-process target #4, ErrorCode ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING / 409).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy DOES hard-gate discharge on uncleared bills (throws InvalidOperationException 'Patient have uncleared bills'), so the *behaviour* (hard reject, not a worklist filter) is faithful and correctly flagged as a hard precondition in the doc (line 93). BUT the legacy implementation is NOT a denormalised billsCleared flag: get_discharge_summary iterates PatientInvoice -> PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill.getStatus() at request time and rejects if ANY bill status equals 'UNPAID' OR 'VERIFIED' (PatientResource.java:5342-5357). It is also NOT limited to CASH patients in legacy — the iteration checks all invoice bills regardless of payment class (COVERED insurance bills simply won't be UNPAID/VERIFIED, so they pass naturally). And it is duplicated identically across THREE endpoints: discharge (5342-5357), referral (5593-5603), and deceased (5851-5882, which also checks consultation invoices).
verdicts.correction: (1) The denormalised bills_cleared flag is a NET-NEW modelling choice — acceptable per ADR-0008 rule 4 but must be CR-noted; its correctness depends on the dispatcher computing 'cleared' as NO bill in status UNPAID or VERIFIED (not merely 'balance == 0'). (2) Do not scope the gate to CASH-only; legacy applies it to all admissions (insurance passes because covered bills aren't UNPAID/VERIFIED). (3) Author the gate for all THREE dispositions (discharge/referral/deceased), and include the deceased path's additional consultation-invoice check. (4) 'VERIFIED' as well as 'UNPAID' must count as outstanding.

verdicts.planClaim: DischargePlan has a typed `kind ∈ {DISCHARGE, DECEASED, REFERRAL}` (line 16, 52, 105).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy DischargePlan has NO kind/type/category field. Its fields are free-text clinical strings (history, investigation, management, operationNote, icuAdmissionNote, generalRecommendation) plus `private String status = 'PENDING'` (DischargePlan.java:39-46). Disposition is encoded ONLY by which separate entity exists — DischargePlan vs ReferralPlan vs DeceasedNote — created via distinct endpoints (saveDischargePlan / saveReferralPlan / deceased path, PatientResource.java:5269/5451/5725). There is no single typed-kind entity. This is the SAME class of drift as the inc-06 'typed states/entities asserted over a system that didn't implement them' lesson.
verdicts.correction: Either (a) preserve the legacy three-separate-entity shape (DischargePlan / ReferralPlan / DeceasedNote), OR (b) explicitly document the unified DischargePlan.kind as a NET-NEW re-modelling (CR-tracked) and verify the field-level requirements per kind against the actual legacy entities — DISCHARGE fields = the free-text clinical strings above; DECEASED fields must be confirmed against the legacy DeceasedNote entity (timeOfDeath/causeOfDeath not yet evidenced in this lane); REFERRAL fields against ReferralPlan. Do not assert the typed kind as legacy fidelity.

verdicts.planClaim: Admission states are typed: ADMITTED -> DISCHARGED / DECEASED / REFERRED / TRANSFERRED; WardBed states FREE/OCCUPIED/RESERVED/OUT_OF_SERVICE; DischargePlan PENDING->APPROVED (lines 13, 14, 16, 52).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy Admission.status is a free-text String (Admission.java:45) with observed values PENDING (doAdmission 1719), IN-PROCESS (1959), STOPPED, HELD, SIGNED-OUT — NOT {ADMITTED, DISCHARGED, DECEASED, REFERRED, TRANSFERRED}. The doc's state names do not match legacy: legacy has no 'ADMITTED' (it's PENDING then IN-PROCESS), no 'DISCHARGED' (it's SIGNED-OUT), uses HELD for deceased, and STOPPED as the pre-signout state. WardBed.status legacy values are EMPTY/WAITING/OCCUPIED (WardBed.java:43-44; PatientServiceImpl.java:1707-1711,1961-1962) — NOT FREE/RESERVED/OUT_OF_SERVICE. Typing these as enums is acceptable modernization, but the state-NAME set and transition sequence in the doc diverge from legacy.
verdicts.correction: Map the new enum values to legacy strings explicitly: Admission PENDING->IN-PROCESS->{STOPPED->SIGNED-OUT (discharge/referral) | HELD (deceased)}. WardBed EMPTY->WAITING (on admit start)->OCCUPIED (on IN-PROCESS)->EMPTY (on signout); there is no legacy RESERVED/OUT_OF_SERVICE. Re-modelling to typed enums is ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS only if the value set and transitions reproduce these; the current doc's value set is invented and must be reconciled or CR-noted.

verdicts.planClaim: WardBed is released (status set to free) on any terminal transition; the discharge frees the bed (line 14, exact-process target #1).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy DOES set WardBed status to EMPTY on sign-out (PatientResource.java:5359-5383). BUT note a legacy quirk the doc silently 'fixes': legacy NEVER closes the AdmissionBed on discharge — the discharge paths only set WardBed=EMPTY and never set AdmissionBed status CLOSED/closedAt (confirmed absent by grep). AdmissionBed is closed ONLY inside the UpdatePatient accrual rollover (UpdatePatient.java:276-278,297-298). So a discharged admission is left with its final AdmissionBed still OPENED. The doc collapses WardBed and AdmissionBed into one 'bed released' concept.
verdicts.correction: Clarify the two distinct legacy concepts: WardBed (the physical bed, set EMPTY on signout) vs AdmissionBed (the per-occupancy billing link, left OPENED at discharge in legacy). If the new build closes AdmissionBed at discharge, that is a CR-tracked cleanup of a legacy bug; if it must reproduce legacy exactly, the AdmissionBed stays OPENED. Decide and document; do not silently merge them.

verdicts.planClaim: WardTransfer is an immutable record of every ward-to-ward move; ward-to-ward transfer updates bed assignment without changing admission state; endpoint POST .../transfer-ward (lines 14, 15, 32, 52, 104).
verdicts.evidence: The ward-accrual-billing ground-truth findings do not cover a ward-to-ward transfer mechanism. The findings explicitly note (absences) that separate MAR/WardTransfer/FluidBalance entities were 'out of lane scope but confirmed absent from all ward-charge/discharge code examined' — i.e. no WardTransfer entity appears in the doAdmission, accrual, or discharge paths reviewed, but the lane did not exhaustively search for a transfer endpoint elsewhere. Given the inc-06/inc-08 pattern (separate entities that turn out to be columns or absent) and the explicit absence note, default skepticism applies.
verdicts.correction: Raise a legacy-analyst query (the doc itself lists this as a supporting-agent task on line 100): confirm whether legacy supports ward-to-ward transfer at all, and if so whether it is a typed WardTransfer entity, a re-admission, or absent. Do NOT author WardTransfer stories as legacy reproduction until confirmed; treat as likely NET-NEW pending evidence.

verdicts.planClaim: Second-approver discharge gate: DischargePlan.approve() rejects if approvedBy == createdBy (M17, lines 16, 52, 68, 86, 105; ErrorCode SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN / 422).
verdicts.evidence: No legacy basis in the ward-accrual-billing findings for a two-approver / approvedBy!=createdBy gate on discharge. Legacy DischargePlan has only `status` (PENDING/APPROVED-style String) and no approvedBy/createdBy approver-distinction fields evidenced (DischargePlan.java:39-46). The legacy discharge transition (STOPPED->SIGNED-OUT) is driven by the bills-cleared gate inside get_discharge_summary, with dischargedBy stamped from the plan — there is no second-distinct-approver precondition shown. The doc itself attributes this to 'M17' (a prior-attempt defect/finding), and loadDischargeList filters plans by status IN {PENDING, APPROVED} only. This is the inc-06-class pattern of asserting a control the legacy did not implement.
verdicts.correction: Flag the second-approver (approvedBy != createdBy) gate as NET-NEW scope (segregation-of-duties improvement), CR-tracked and labelled as such — NOT as legacy fidelity. Acceptance criteria must state it is a net-new requirement. Separately confirm with legacy-analyst whether legacy DischargePlan.status ever moves to APPROVED via a distinct user and what privilege governed it; the doc's privilege code DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE is unverified against legacy.

verdicts.planClaim: Privilege codes ADMISSION-CREATE, ADMISSION-DISCHARGE, NURSING-CHART-WRITE, MAR-WRITE, DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE seeded in inc-01 (line 56, 112, 113).
verdicts.evidence: The ward-accrual-billing ground-truth findings do not enumerate the legacy privilege codes governing admission/discharge/ward billing. The discharge gates examined (PatientResource.java:5342-5882) reject on bill status, not on a named privilege within this lane's evidence. Given the inc-08 lesson where invented privilege codes / '177' were phantom/unratified, these specific code strings must be verified against the legacy privilege/role tables before being asserted as seedable.
verdicts.correction: Verify each privilege code string against the legacy privilege catalogue (the doc assigns this to legacy-analyst on line 100: 'confirm second-approver privilege code names'). Until confirmed, treat the exact code strings as proposed/NET-NEW naming, not legacy reproduction. Do not let @PreAuthorize gates assert privilege codes that do not exist in the legacy RBAC seed.

verdicts.planClaim: Document date / ward-day businessDate must reflect Africa/Dar_es_Salaam (EAT) local calendar date, not UTC (line 80, exact-process target #9).
verdicts.evidence: The ward-accrual-billing findings do not cover the legacy date/timezone handling for ward bills (openedAt/closedAt are Long/LocalDateTime in UpdatePatient/AdmissionBed but the lane did not extract the timezone semantics). The legacy accrual keys on openedAt+24h intervals, not a calendar 'businessDate' at all — so introducing an EAT calendar businessDate is part of the NET-NEW cron-accrual model (see JOB-001 verdict), making this an attribute of net-new design rather than a verified legacy behaviour.
verdicts.correction: Tie this AC to the NET-NEW accrual model rather than to legacy fidelity. Separately confirm legacy timezone handling with legacy-analyst (ADR-0009 §7 is cited but ADR-0009 contains no ward references). The notion of a per-night 'businessDate' is itself new since legacy uses rolling 24h-from-openedAt intervals.

newDriftFound: LEGACY-ACCRUAL-IS-A-POLLING-THREAD-NOT-SINGLE-CHARGE: Both the planning doc (line 5, 66) AND its governing ADR-0018 (context line 15) mis-state the legacy as 'one flat charge at admission with no re-accrual'. Ground truth shows legacy DOES accrue per-24h via the UpdatePatient Runnable polling Thread (MainApplication.java:160-184, UpdatePatient.java:258-334). This is a factual error propagated into the architecture decision record itself and should be corrected upstream, because the M23-fix justification ('single-charge model has no answer for day-3 running bill') is partly moot — legacy already accrued, just via a non-Spring thread that silently stops once the admission leaves IN-PROCESS/STOPPED.

ACCRUAL-STOPS-ON-STATUS-CHANGE: Legacy accrual only runs while admission.status is IN-PROCESS or STOPPED (UpdatePatient.java:258-262). A cron job iterating 'each ADMITTED admission' (ADR-0018 line 61) will accrue under a different status predicate than legacy, and legacy stops accruing the moment a discharge/referral plan flips status to STOPPED-then-SIGNED-OUT or HELD (deceased). Parity tests must reconcile WHEN accrual stops, not just the per-night amount.

FIRST-BILL-UNPAID-vs-ACCRUED-BILL-VERIFIED: Legacy admission-time ward bill is UNPAID but every accrued ward bill is VERIFIED (PatientServiceImpl.java:1760 vs UpdatePatient.java:304-311). The doc models all ward lines uniformly; the discharge gate keys on BOTH UNPAID and VERIFIED, so this status distinction is load-bearing and currently unmodelled.

WARDTYPEINSURANCEPLAN.ACTIVE-FLAG-IGNORED: Legacy insurance ward eligibility uses findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan,true) and never checks the active flag (WardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.java:40; WardTypeInsurancePlan.java:43-44) — an inactive-but-covered plan stays eligible. This is a latent legacy quirk the doc neither reproduces nor flags; needs a legacy-analyst decision (preserve vs CR-fix).

TOP-UP-WARD-BILL-UNMODELLED: Legacy creates a supplementary 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' PatientBill (UNPAID) for WardType.price minus eligiblePlan.price when the eligible plan differs from the patient's own and the difference > 0 (PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1897). Completely absent from the doc's pricing model.

ADMISSIONBED-NOT-CLOSED-AT-DISCHARGE: Legacy never closes the AdmissionBed on discharge (only sets WardBed=EMPTY); the final AdmissionBed is left OPENED (absence confirmed by grep; closure happens only in UpdatePatient rollover). The doc's 'bed released on terminal transition' collapses two distinct legacy concepts (WardBed vs AdmissionBed) and silently 'fixes' a legacy leak without a CR.

DISCHARGE-GATE-NOT-CASH-ONLY: The doc scopes the bills-cleared discharge block to CASH patients (lines 52, 70, 106); legacy applies the UNPAID/VERIFIED bill-status reject to ALL admissions across discharge/referral/deceased endpoints (PatientResource.java:5342-5357, 5593-5603, 5851-5882). Insurance patients pass only because covered bills are not UNPAID/VERIFIED, not because the gate is CASH-scoped.

extract.lane: RBAC (@PreAuthorize) on inpatient endpoints + admission numbering
extract.summary: In the legacy Zana HMIS, ALL inpatient/admission/discharge/referral/deceased-note endpoints in PatientResource.java are effectively UNGATED: every @PreAuthorize on them is commented out (prefixed with //), and the nursing/MAR/consumable-chart and medicine-issue/give endpoints have no @PreAuthorize annotation at all. Only the OUTPATIENT patient-registration and consultation endpoints carry ACTIVE @PreAuthorize using the codes PATIENT-ALL, PATIENT-CREATE, PATIENT-UPDATE (7 endpoints total). This is reinforced at the HTTP layer: SecurityConfig has .anyRequest().authenticated() commented out, and CustomAuthorizationFilter lets any request with no Authorization header pass straight through, so an endpoint without an active @PreAuthorize is reachable without authentication. The commented-out inpatient annotations use a STALE shorthand (PATIENT-A/PATIENT-C/PATIENT-U) that does not match any defined privilege constant. Admission has NO number/prefix/sequence scheme whatsoever — the only identifier is the auto-increment DB id (GenerationType.IDENTITY); status is a free-text String (not a typed enum) cycling through PENDING -> IN-PROCESS -> STOPPED/HELD -> SIGNED-OUT.

findings.topic: Inpatient endpoint RBAC — do_admission is UNGATED
findings.behaviour: The POST /patients/do_admission endpoint has its @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (//@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-A','PATIENT-C','PATIENT-U')")). No active authorization annotation. Note the commented code uses stale short codes PATIENT-A/C/U that do not match defined privileges.

findings.topic: Discharge endpoint RBAC — save_discharge_plan UNGATED
findings.behaviour: POST /patients/save_discharge_plan @PreAuthorize is commented out. The method itself flips admission.setStatus("STOPPED") and dischargePlan.setStatus("PENDING") with no role check.

findings.topic: Discharge/referral/deceased endpoints all UNGATED
findings.behaviour: Every one of load_discharge_plan(5293), load_discharge_list(5313), get_discharge_summary(5330), save_referral_plan(5393), load_referral_plan(5537), load_referral_list(5563), get_referral_summary(5579), save_deceased_note(5694), load_deceased_note(5778), load_deceased_list(5822), get_deceased_summary(5838) has its @PreAuthorize commented out with the stale PATIENT-A/C/U shorthand.

findings.topic: Nursing/MAR/consumable chart endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize at all
findings.behaviour: save_patient_dressing_chart(2135), save_patient_consumable_chart(2152), save_patient_observation_chart(2169), save_patient_prescription_chart(2186), save_patient_nursing_chart(2203), save_patient_nursing_progress_note(2220), save_patient_nursing_care_plan(2237) carry NO @PreAuthorize line (neither active nor commented). The grep of all 34 @PreAuthorize occurrences shows none attached to these mappings.

findings.topic: Medicine issue/give (MAR-adjacent) endpoints UNGATED
findings.behaviour: POST /patients/issue_medicine (3199) and GET /patients/give_medicine (6212) have no @PreAuthorize annotation. give_medicine area only shows commented //@PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('PRODUCT-CREATE')") near attachment endpoints, not on the medicine endpoints themselves.

findings.topic: Only OUTPATIENT endpoints carry ACTIVE @PreAuthorize
