findings.behaviour: The only 7 active (non-commented) @PreAuthorize in the entire 6455-line file are on outpatient flows: register(289)='PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE'; update(379) and change_type(399)='PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-UPDATE'; do_consultation(509), switch_to_normal_consultation(548), cancel_consultation(606), free_consultation(681)='PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE'. change_payment_type(311) is commented out.

findings.topic: Privilege codes are a loose String constant, not an enum
findings.behaviour: Privilege codes derive from a space-delimited String constant: PATIENT = "PATIENT-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE". The active annotation tokens PATIENT-ALL/PATIENT-CREATE/PATIENT-UPDATE match this convention; the commented-out PATIENT-A/PATIENT-C/PATIENT-U tokens match nothing and are stale dead code.

findings.topic: No HTTP-layer blanket authentication — anyRequest().authenticated() is commented out
findings.behaviour: SecurityConfig.configure(HttpSecurity) builds authorizeRequests() with permitAll() for login/swagger but the line //.anyRequest().authenticated(); is COMMENTED OUT. Method security is enabled via @EnableGlobalMethodSecurity(prePostEnabled=true), so the ONLY access control is active @PreAuthorize. Endpoints without one are unprotected at the HTTP layer.

findings.topic: Authorization filter does NOT reject anonymous requests
findings.behaviour: CustomAuthorizationFilter: if the Authorization header is null or not 'Bearer ', the else branch simply calls filterChain.doFilter(request,response) and lets the request proceed. No 401/403 is raised for missing token. JWT secret is hardcoded HMAC256('secret') and the DB token-revocation check (lines 82-90) is commented out.

findings.topic: Admission numbering — NO number, prefix, or sequence
findings.behaviour: Admission entity has fields id(IDENTITY), paymentType, membershipNo, status, and FKs/forensic columns only. There is NO 'no'/'number'/'admissionNo' field. doAdmission() never generates any code — it sets status and saves; the sole identifier is the auto-increment DB id. status is a free-text String, not a typed enum.

findings.topic: Admission status state machine (free-text String values)
findings.behaviour: doAdmission sets status='PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl:1719). On consultation sign-in it becomes 'IN-PROCESS' (PatientServiceImpl:1959). save_discharge_plan sets 'STOPPED' (PatientResource:5269). Sign-out transitions STOPPED->'SIGNED-OUT' (PatientResource:5361,5612,5892). Other observed values: 'HELD' and back to 'IN-PROCESS' (PatientResource:5721,5725). No 'DISCHARGED' literal sets admission status; sign-out terminal value is 'SIGNED-OUT'.

findings.topic: Inpatient worklists are status FILTERS, not RBAC gates
findings.behaviour: get_doctor_inpatient_list returns admissionRepository.findAllByStatusIn(['IN-PROCESS','STOPPED']); get_nurse_inpatient_list returns findAllByStatus('IN-PROCESS'). Neither has @PreAuthorize — visibility is governed purely by admission status, not by role/privilege.

findings.topic: do_admission service guards (hard rejects, not RBAC)
findings.behaviour: doAdmission hard-rejects via InvalidOperationException if patient already has an admission in ['PENDING','IN-PROCESS'] (PatientResource:5197-5200), if bed not active (PatientServiceImpl:1703-1705), or if bed status != 'EMPTY' (PatientServiceImpl:1707-1709). These are business guards inside the service, independent of any privilege check.

absences.expected: Active @PreAuthorize privilege gating on inpatient (admission/discharge/nursing/MAR/consumable) endpoints
absences.searched: Grepped all 34 @PreAuthorize occurrences in api/PatientResource.java (lines 289-6144) and cross-listed against all @PostMapping/@GetMapping. Every inpatient-related annotation is commented out; nursing/MAR/consumable/medicine endpoints have none at all. Only 7 outpatient endpoints (register/update/change_type/consultation flows) are actively gated.

absences.searched: Read entire domain/Admission.java (84 lines) — no number/no/code field, id uses GenerationType.IDENTITY. Read doAdmission() in service/PatientServiceImpl.java:1701-1790 — no number generation logic. No separate sequence/counter entity referenced for admissions.

absences.expected: A typed enum for admission status / DischargePlan.kind
absences.searched: Admission.status is declared 'private String status;' (Admission.java:45) — free text. Grepped setStatus across PatientServiceImpl and PatientResource: values are bare String literals (PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT). absences.expected: HTTP-layer blanket authentication requiring a valid token for all endpoints
absences.searched: Read security/SecurityConfig.java fully — .anyRequest().authenticated() is commented out (line 89). Read filter/CustomAuthorizationFilter.java fully — missing-header branch (105-107) passes the request through with no rejection. No effective baseline auth gate exists.

absences.expected: 177-code IAM privilege catalogue referenced by inpatient endpoints
absences.searched: Grepped PATIENT-* tokens across the api tree — only defined in security/Object_.java:23 as a space-delimited String constant ('PATIENT-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE'). Consistent with the ratified ~35-code IAM record; no large structured 177-code privilege set is wired into these endpoints.

verdicts.planClaim: Inc-01 dependency (line 56): privilege codes ADMISSION-CREATE, ADMISSION-DISCHARGE, NURSING-CHART-WRITE, MAR-WRITE, DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE 'seeded before this increment's @PreAuthorize gates can be tested end-to-end.' (treated as a coherent set of legacy-derived codes)
verdicts.evidence: Mostly invented codes presented as a set. Legacy privileges are generated by reflection over Object_.java constants (MainApplication.java:251-295): object = substring before first '-', operations = tokens after last '-', emitting OBJECT-OPERATION. A cleanup loop (MainApplication.java:306-338) then DELETES any privilege whose operation suffix is not in the canonical Operation set, which is exactly {ACCESS, ALL, CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE, CANCEL, REJECT, VERIFY, APPROVE, ARCHIVE, ALLOW} (Operation.java:26-37). There is NO 'DISCHARGE' and NO 'WRITE' operation, and no 'MAR' or 'DISCHARGE-PLAN'/'NURSING-CHART' object constant (Object_.java:23-66 defines ADMISSION='ADMISSION-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE', WARD, NURSING='NURSING', NURSING_SERVICE='NURSING_SERVICE-ACCESS'). Therefore: ADMISSION-DISCHARGE, NURSING-CHART-WRITE, MAR-WRITE, DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE have no legacy basis and would be auto-purged even if seeded. Only ADMISSION-CREATE is legitimately derivable. The recurring '177-code catalogue' framing is absent; the real seed is the small reflected set (~35 codes ratified).
verdicts.correction: Split the claim. ADMISSION-CREATE is legacy-valid (keep). ADMISSION-DISCHARGE, NURSING-CHART-WRITE, MAR-WRITE, DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE are net-new codes with no legacy basis: either (a) reuse the legacy convention codes the inpatient surface would have carried (ADMISSION-CREATE/UPDATE, NURSING_SERVICE-ACCESS, plus WARD-* for ward ops) or (b) register the new fine-grained codes in the change-request register as a deliberate RBAC-hardening deviation. Do not present them as legacy-reproduced. Note: the inpatient surface was UNGATED in legacy (see below), so ALL of this gating is a CR-tracked improvement, not a reproduction.

verdicts.planClaim: Implicit premise of the RBAC dependency and DoD line 113: the legacy inpatient/admission/discharge/nursing/MAR surface was @PreAuthorize-gated, so this increment 'reproduces' that gating with seeded privilege codes.
verdicts.evidence: The entire inpatient surface is effectively UNGATED in legacy. do_admission (PatientResource.java:5183-5184) and save_discharge_plan (5253-5254) have their @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT using stale shorthand PATIENT-A/C/U that matches no defined privilege; the discharge/referral/deceased endpoints (5293,5313,5330,5393,5537,5563,5579,5694,5778,5822,5838) are likewise commented out. The nursing/MAR/consumable chart endpoints carry NO @PreAuthorize at all (2135,2152,2169,2186,2203,2220,2237), as do issue_medicine (3199) and give_medicine (6212). Only 7 OUTPATIENT endpoints are actively gated (register 289, update 379, change_type 399, do_consultation 509, etc.) using PATIENT-ALL/PATIENT-CREATE/PATIENT-UPDATE. This is reinforced at the HTTP layer: SecurityConfig .anyRequest().authenticated() is commented out (SecurityConfig.java:89) and CustomAuthorizationFilter passes header-less requests straight through (CustomAuthorizationFilter.java:105-107). So gating the inpatient endpoints is a NET-NEW security posture, not a reproduction.
verdicts.correction: DoD line 113 and the Inc-01 dependency must state that inpatient @PreAuthorize gating is a deliberate security-hardening deviation from legacy (legacy inpatient endpoints were ungated; only outpatient register/consultation flows were gated). Log this as a change request (consistent with the engagement's net-new tamper-evident-audit/RBAC hardening posture). Acceptance criteria for these gates must NOT be written as 'reproduces legacy behaviour.'

verdicts.planClaim: DoD line 112: approver worklist GET /inpatient/admissions/discharge-plans?status=PENDING is 'gated by DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE privilege.'
verdicts.evidence: There is no DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE (or DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE) privilege in legacy. Object_.java has no DISCHARGE_PLAN object constant; 'APPROVE' is a valid Operation (Operation.java:35) but only attaches to objects whose constant lists it (e.g., LAB_TEST, PAYROLL). The legacy discharge endpoints (e.g., load_discharge_plan PatientResource.java:5293, get_discharge_summary 5330) were ungated entirely, and the second-approver concept itself is the M17 NET-NEW gap, not legacy behaviour. There is no legacy worklist-by-privilege gate to reproduce.
verdicts.correction: Mark DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE as a net-new privilege tied to the net-new M17 second-approver gate; register in the change-request log. If aligning to legacy naming convention, the closest valid forms are ADMISSION-APPROVE (would require adding APPROVE to the ADMISSION constant) or a new APPROVE-bearing object — either way it is net-new and must be flagged, not described as gating an existing legacy flow.

verdicts.planClaim: Privilege code ADMISSION-CREATE (used to gate POST /inpatient/admissions).
verdicts.evidence: ADMISSION-CREATE is a genuinely legacy-derivable token: Object_.java:25 defines ADMISSION = 'ADMISSION-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE', which the seed loop (MainApplication.java:281-293) expands to ADMISSION-ALL, ADMISSION-CREATE, ADMISSION-READ, ADMISSION-UPDATE, and CREATE survives the Operation cleanup (Operation.java:28). HOWEVER, the legacy do_admission endpoint itself was UNGATED (PatientResource.java:5183-5184, commented out), so although the privilege CODE existed in the catalogue, it was never actually enforced on admission. Applying it now is a faithful use of a real legacy code applied to a previously-ungated endpoint.
verdicts.correction: Keep ADMISSION-CREATE (it is a real legacy privilege). Add a note that the legacy admission endpoint was not actually gated by it (commented-out @PreAuthorize), so enforcing it is a CR-tracked hardening of a real code, not a behavioural reproduction.

verdicts.planClaim: Admission identity / numbering: the doc uses a uid-based identifier (e.g., GET /inpatient/admissions/uid/{uid}) and asserts no admission number/prefix/sequence scheme.
verdicts.evidence: Confirmed against legacy: domain/Admission.java:38-84 has only id (GenerationType.IDENTITY at line 40), paymentType, membershipNo, status, FKs (patient/visit/insurancePlan/wardBed) and forensic columns; there is NO no/number/admissionNo field, and doAdmission (PatientServiceImpl.java:1701-1748) generates no code. The planning doc correctly does NOT invent an admission numbering scheme. The modern uid surrogate is a re-modelling that preserves the legacy 'identifier = surrogate key, no human-facing number' behaviour.

verdicts.planClaim: Admission state machine modelled as a typed enum: ADMITTED -> DISCHARGED / DECEASED / REFERRED / TRANSFERRED (lines 13, 52, 64).
verdicts.evidence: Legacy admission status is a free-text String (Admission.java:45 'private String status;'), not a typed enum, and the actual literal values differ from the doc's enum: doAdmission sets 'PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl.java:1719); consultation sign-in sets 'IN-PROCESS' (1959); save_discharge_plan sets 'STOPPED' (PatientResource.java:5269); sign-out terminal value is 'SIGNED-OUT' (5361,5611-5612,5892); 'HELD' and back to 'IN-PROCESS' also observed (5721-5725). There is NO 'DISCHARGED', 'DECEASED', 'REFERRED', or 'TRANSFERRED' literal in the legacy admission status; the terminal value is uniformly 'SIGNED-OUT'. The doc's terminal kinds are derived from DischargePlan.kind (also net-new typing), not from admission status values.
verdicts.correction: Modelling admission status as a typed enum is an acceptable modernisation, but the enum VALUES must map to legacy semantics and the doc must document the mapping: legacy PENDING->(modern) e.g. PENDING/ADMITTED, IN-PROCESS->ADMITTED/ACTIVE, STOPPED->discharge-pending, SIGNED-OUT->terminal (covers DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED in legacy, which were NOT separate admission states — they are distinguished only by which plan was filed). HELD must not be silently dropped. State the legacy literal set (PENDING, IN-PROCESS, STOPPED, HELD, SIGNED-OUT) and the modern enum as a re-modelling with an explicit value-mapping table; do not present DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED/TRANSFERRED as legacy admission-status values.

newDriftFound: Legacy privilege purge loop (MainApplication.java:306-338) actively DELETES any seeded privilege whose operation suffix is not in the canonical Operation set (ACCESS/ALL/CREATE/READ/UPDATE/DELETE/CANCEL/REJECT/VERIFY/APPROVE/ARCHIVE/ALLOW). This means any *-WRITE or *-DISCHARGE privilege the doc proposes to seed would be auto-destroyed on startup under legacy conventions — the doc's proposed codes are not just absent, they are convention-incompatible. The fresh build needs an explicit, CR-approved IAM convention decision (whether to extend the operation vocabulary with WRITE/DISCHARGE) before these codes can persist.

DischargePlan terminal disposition is NOT carried on admission status in legacy: legacy admission status terminates uniformly at 'SIGNED-OUT' regardless of whether a discharge, referral, or deceased note was filed. The distinction lives in which plan/note record exists (save_discharge_plan / save_referral_plan / save_deceased_note), not in a DischargePlan.kind discriminator nor in admission state. The doc's kind-typed DischargePlan and the four-way terminal admission enum together over-type a legacy design that used one free-text status plus separate plan/note save endpoints.

Legacy 'HELD' admission status (PatientResource.java:5721-5725) is entirely absent from the planning doc's state machine. If any legacy hold/resume behaviour must be reproduced, it is currently unmapped and would be silently dropped.

The legacy inpatient/MAR worklists are status FILTERS, not RBAC gates: get_doctor_inpatient_list = findAllByStatusIn(['IN-PROCESS','STOPPED']), get_nurse_inpatient_list = findAllByStatus('IN-PROCESS') (PatientResource.java:3610-3633), with no @PreAuthorize. The doc's role-scoped/privilege-gated nurse worklist is a net-new access-control layer over what was purely a status-based visibility query — worth a CR note so it is not mistaken for legacy reproduction.

synthesis.verdict: inc-07 (Inpatient & Nursing) is a REAL full ground-up build, NOT a verify-and-patch. The target module com.otapp.hmis.inpatient is a bare stub (package-info.java only, "@ApplicationModule, Stub for increment 00" — confirmed in-repo) with no domain/application/api. This is the same posture as inc-08: there is nothing to reconcile against in the new codebase, so the entire admission/discharge/nursing/consumable/ward-charge surface must be built from scratch, EXTRACTED from legacy com.orbix.api, under "modern design, exact process". HOWEVER, the planning doc that fed this increment has drifted heavily toward an idealized clinical-safety design that the legacy never implemented. The WORST phantoms (each verified by file:line as absent or contradicted in legacy): (1) WardTransfer entity + POST /transfer-ward + a TRANSFERRED admission state — legacy has NO ward-to-ward transfer at all (both bed FKs are @JoinColumn(updatable=false); only *Transfer class is ConsultationTransfer, unrelated). (2) A net-new closed-loop MedicationAdministration/MAR aggregate framed as mandatory exact-process M15 "fix" — legacy has NO MAR; the closest artefact is the free-text PatientPrescriptionChart (dosage/output/remark). (3) ConsumableStockBalance.decrementForIssue as an inc-08 dependency — this exact name exists nowhere in HMSCLEAN2 Java (confirmed grep: zero source matches), AND legacy consumable issue touches NO stock entity whatsoever (billing-only event). (4) A WardDayAccrualJob as a Spring @Scheduled+ShedLock midnight cron presented as exact-process — there is zero scheduling infra in HMSCLEAN2 (confirmed grep) and legacy accrual is a hand-rolled 5-min polling Thread keyed on rolling-24h-from-openedAt, not a calendar cron. (5) A typed DischargePlan.kind discriminator + a second-approver SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN gate + invented privilege codes (MAR-WRITE, NURSING-CHART-WRITE, DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE, "177"-style catalogue) — all net-new, with the discharge approver in legacy ALWAYS copied from the creator (approvedBy == createdBy). Net verdict: build inc-07 as a genuine full module, but first strip the doc's phantoms and re-baseline against the corrected legacy model below. Recommend splitting into 07a/07b/07c and parking the safety improvements (MAR, second-approver, deceased-readmit guard, ward-transfer, cron-accrual idempotency) as explicit CRs distinct from exact-process baseline.
alreadyBuilt: STOCK-DECREMENT ENGINES (inc-08, REUSE — do NOT rebuild; doc's named dependency is WRONG): There is NO ConsumableStockBalance and NO decrementForIssue anywhere (confirmed zero Java matches in HMSCLEAN2). (StockService.java:69-85, hard negative-stock gate at :78 throwing InsufficientStockException 422, then FEFO StockBatch walk + append-only StockMovement OUT) and inventory/application/StoreStockService.decrementFefo(...) (StoreStockService.java:83-95, same hard-gate-first at :89). Both are package-private to their .application package (NO @NamedInterface), so inc-07 CANNOT call them cross-module as-is.

synthesis.groundTruthModel:
CORRECTED LEGACY MODEL (supersedes the planning doc where they conflict; all cites are legacy com.orbix.api unless noted).

ADMISSION is a single flat JPA entity (admissions table); status is a FREE-TEXT String (Admission.java:45), NOT a typed enum. The real two-phase lifecycle: PENDING -> IN-PROCESS -> {STOPPED -> SIGNED-OUT (discharge/referral)} | {HELD -> SIGNED-OUT (deceased)}. doAdmission (PatientServiceImpl.java:1701-2021) creates the admission PENDING with bed WAITING, an UNPAID ward-bed PatientBill priced at WardType.price (billItem 'Bed', desc 'Ward Bed / Room'), an AdmissionBed(OPENED), and flips patient.type=INPATIENT. ACTIVATION is PAYMENT-GATED: ONLY the full-insurance-cover branch (:1950-1963) activates to IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED immediately; CASH and insurance-with-topup stay PENDING until the ward-bed bill is PAID, at which point PatientBillResource.java:352-365 promotes ALL the patient's PENDING admissions to IN-PROCESS / beds OCCUPIED (and signs out IN-PROCESS consultations). PENDING admissions appear in NO worklist; charting is hard-rejected 'Admission not verified' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2062-2069). Admit guards: existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission rejects (PatientResource.java:5194-5200); bed must be active+EMPTY (PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1711); NO deceased guard.

WARD-DAY ACCRUAL EXISTS in legacy but as a hand-rolled background Thread, NOT a single flat charge and NOT a cron: UpdatePatient implements Runnable, started via new Thread(...).start() in MainApplication.java:160-184, loops while(true){sleep(300000)} (5 min). For each {IN-PROCESS,STOPPED} admission whose OPENED AdmissionBed is >=24h old (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt,now)>=24, UpdatePatient.java:291-292) it CLOSES that AdmissionBed and creates a NEW VERIFIED ward-bed PatientBill + a NEW OPENED AdmissionBed (:294-334). De-dup branch closes multiple OPENED beds and 'continue's. Accrual STOPS once status leaves IN-PROCESS/STOPPED. STATUS DISTINCTION IS LOAD-BEARING: admission-time bill is UNPAID (PatientServiceImpl.java:1760), accrued bills are VERIFIED (UpdatePatient.java:304-311), insurance bills COVERED. Total = (1 + N) x WardType.price where N = completed rolling-24h intervals while the thread ran.

INSURANCE WARD PRICING is richer than a plan-keyed lookup: doAdmission selects WardTypeInsurancePlan via findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan,true) by a max-price-OR-exact-plan-match loop (PatientServiceImpl.java:1795-1819); the .active flag is IGNORED (an inactive-but-covered plan stays eligible — WardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.java:40). If the eligible plan differs from the patient's own AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0, a supplementary 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' UNPAID PatientBill is created for the difference (:1880-1897).

DISCHARGE/REFERRAL/DECEASED are THREE SEPARATE legacy entities/tables (discharge_plans / referral_plans / deceased_notes), each with a free-text String status defaulting PENDING -> only APPROVED. There is NO typed kind discriminator; disposition = which entity exists + resulting Patient.type. DischargePlan is admission-only with flat narrative fields (history/investigation/management/operationNote/icuAdmissionNote/generalRecommendation, DischargePlan.java:38-46), NOT validated mandatory at create. ReferralPlan AND DeceasedNote support BOTH admission AND consultation (outpatient) flows (XOR on admission/consultation id). ReferralPlan has a @ManyToOne FK to ExternalMedicalProvider (nullable=false) — a real provider entity, NOT free-text. DeceasedNote validates patientSummary + causeOfDeath mandatory and uses split user-entered LocalDate date_ + LocalTime time_ (DeceasedNote.java:40-46), NOT a server clock.

BILLS-CLEARED DISCHARGE GATE is a HARD inline service reject (throws), NOT a flag and NOT a worklist filter, duplicated across all three summaries: get_discharge_summary (PatientResource.java:5342-5357), get_referral_summary (:5593-5603), get_deceased_summary (:5851-5882) iterate PatientInvoice->PatientInvoiceDetail->PatientBill.getStatus() and reject if ANY equals 'UNPAID' OR 'VERIFIED'. There is NO billsCleared field (confirmed zero matches). The gate applies to ALL admissions (NOT CASH-only); insurance passes only because COVERED bills aren't UNPAID/VERIFIED. On pass it APPROVES all invoices as a side effect, then (only if status==STOPPED/HELD) sets SIGNED-OUT, frees WardBed to EMPTY, resets Patient.type=OUTPATIENT/paymentType=CASH/insurancePlan=null (deceased sets type=DECEASED). The deceased path frees the bed EARLY at the HELD transition (saveDeceasedNote, :5719-5732) and does NOT stamp dischargedBy/On/At. Approver ALWAYS = creator (approvedBy=createdBy copied, :5378-5381) — NO second-approver gate. AdmissionBed is NEVER closed at discharge (legacy leak — only closed in the accrual rollover).

NURSING CHARTS are FIVE flat entities, NOT six: PatientVital, PatientNursingChart, PatientNursingCarePlan, PatientNursingProgressNote, PatientDressingChart. Fluid-balance (drainageOutput/fluidIntake/urineOutput) and care-activity (feeding/changingPosition/bedBathing/randomBloodSugar/fullBloodSugar) are free-text String COLUMNS on PatientNursingChart (PatientNursingChart.java:38-45) — NOT separate FluidBalance/CareActivity entities. Progress note is a single free-text 'note' (no kind enum). Care plan is four free-text strings (no ACTIVE/RESOLVED/CANCELLED lifecycle). Chart/note/care-plan saves are admission-ONLY inserts (hard-reject outpatient/outsider), require a Nurse, and gate on admission status (PENDING reject 'Admission not verified'; IN-PROCESS proceed; else 'Patient already signed off' — PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635). All charts are deletable ONLY within a 24h window (PatientResource.java:3135-3138). PatientVital is a SINGLE-ROW upsert per context (last-wins overwrite, PatientResource.java:1043-1114) with a free-text status EMPTY->PENDING->SUBMITTED submit state machine and a context-exclusivity gate; vitals support consultation/nonConsultation/admission (NOT admission-only) and uniquely have NO patient/nurse FK.

PatientDressingChart is a BILLING record, not a clinical wound chart: NO WoundStatus enum, NO wound field. It has qty/paymentType/membershipNo + mandatory @OneToOne PatientBill + mandatory @ManyToOne ProcedureType, gated by a Dressing masterdata marker ('Procedure type is not listed as dressing'). savePatientDressingChart writes money (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245): UNPAID bill at ProcedureType.price; INSURANCE/admission -> ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan covered -> COVERED + PatientInvoiceDetail under a PENDING invoice; else admission -> VERIFIED. Delete raises a PENDING credit note + cascade.

CONSUMABLE issue is a BILLING-ONLY event with ZERO inventory effect. Consumable (Consumable.java:35-50) is a @OneToOne pointer to Medicine (master-data marker, NO qty/batch/balance); Medicine has price but NO stock field. savePatientConsumableChart (PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475) guards Medicine exists / qty>0 / Medicine registered as consumable ('Medicine is not listed as consumable') / nurse present / exactly-one context / admission status; it creates a PatientBill and persists PatientConsumableChart with free-text status hard-coded 'NOT-GIVEN' (never advanced anywhere — the only 'GIVEN' is a read-only delete guard). NO stock entity read or decremented; NO negative-stock gate; NO locking; NO FEFO. Bill status trichotomy: UNPAID (cash, :2307-2319) | COVERED (MedicineInsurancePlan covered=true, :2321-2395) | VERIFIED (cash-on-admission, :2396-2465); invoiceDetail.qty is hard-coded to 1 in BOTH branches (quirk).

MAR: there is NO MedicationAdministration/MAR entity anywhere (whole-repo grep zero matches). 'GIVEN' means PHARMACY-DISPENSED (issueMedicine, PatientResource.java:3230, 6259), never administered-to-patient. There is no dispensedAt field, no prescription-LINE concept (Prescription IS the line, Long IDENTITY id, no uid), no route reference (route is free-text on the order), no patientResponse. Inpatient med tracking = (1) Prescription order + String status pharmacy issue, (2) PatientPrescriptionChart free-text dosing notes, (3) PatientConsumableChart billed consumables.

ADMISSION NUMBERING: NONE. Admission has only id (GenerationType.IDENTITY); no no/number/admissionNo field, no sequence. uid surrogate in the modern build is fine.

RBAC: the ENTIRE inpatient surface was UNGATED in legacy. do_admission/discharge/referral/deceased @PreAuthorize are all COMMENTED OUT with stale PATIENT-A/C/U shorthand matching no privilege; nursing/MAR/consumable/issue_medicine endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize at all; only 7 OUTPATIENT endpoints are actively gated. SecurityConfig .anyRequest().authenticated() is commented out and the filter passes header-less requests through. Privilege codes are reflected from Object_.java constants (ADMISSION='ADMISSION-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE'); a cleanup loop DELETES any privilege whose operation suffix is not in the canonical Operation set {ACCESS,ALL,CREATE,READ,UPDATE,DELETE,CANCEL,REJECT,VERIFY,APPROVE,ARCHIVE,ALLOW} — so *-WRITE / *-DISCHARGE codes would be auto-purged. ADMISSION-CREATE is the only legacy-valid inpatient privilege code, but it was never actually enforced (commented out).

AUDIT: AuditRecorder (shared.audit, ADR-0007 SHA-256 chained) is the explicit mechanism — NOT Envers (confirmed phantom dep, zero @Audited). DEVICE-FINGERPRINT: confirmed absent everywhere (phantom). Inc-07 must not reproduce either.
drift: WardTransfer entity + POST /transfer-ward + TRANSFERRED admission state + 'WardBed updated on transfer' — PHANTOM. Legacy has NO ward-to-ward/bed-to-bed transfer; both bed FKs are @JoinColumn(updatable=false); only *Transfer class is the unrelated ConsultationTransfer. Park the entire feature as a NET-NEW CR; do NOT write acceptance criteria as legacy reproduction.

MedicationAdministration/MAR mandatory aggregate (M15) with prescriptionLineUid/administeredAt/doseGiven/routeUid/administeredByUsername/patientResponse — PHANTOM. No MAR entity in legacy; 'GIVEN' = pharmacy-dispensed not administered; no dispensedAt field; no prescription-line/uid; route is free-text on order; no patientResponse. Re-label MAR as NET-NEW clinical-safety CR; its golden-master is a feature acceptance test, NOT a parity test (line-116 mislabel).

M15 rationale 'prior build tracked only pharmacy dispensedAt' — DRIFT. Legacy has NO dispensedAt field (grep zero); pharmacy issue is a Prescription.status NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN flip + approvedAt stamp. The premise inventing a non-existent legacy field must be corrected.

ConsumableStockBalance + ConsumableStockService.decrementForIssue inc-08 dependency + PESSIMISTIC @Lock(ADR-0017) decrement in the chart tx — PHANTOM (double). These names exist nowhere in HMSCLEAN2 Java (confirmed zero source matches; only planning .md/.mjs). Legacy consumable issue touches NO stock. Delete the dependency, the pessimistic lock, and ADR-0017 from the consumable lane; consumable issue has NO inc-08 stock dependency.

Concurrent 'last unit' consumable issue -> one success + one 409 STOCK_INSUFFICIENT (DoD concurrency test) — PHANTOM. No stock balance exists, so no 'last unit' and no overdraft semantics to reproduce. Remove the fidelity target; if wanted, it is NET-NEW CR.

ConsumableIssue line fields sourceKind {PHARMACY,STORE} + sourceLocationUid + unitCost snapshot — DRIFT. Legacy PatientConsumableChart has no source-location or unitCost-snapshot concept (price read live from Medicine.price/MedicineInsurancePlan). sourceKind/sourceLocationUid are NET-NEW; unitCost-snapshot is net-new hardening. (qty double->NUMERIC(19,6) is pre-approved data-type modernization — acceptable.)

PatientConsumableChart status as a typed lifecycle advancing to GIVEN at administration — DRIFT. status is free-text hard-coded 'NOT-GIVEN', never advanced; only 'GIVEN' use is a delete guard. No give endpoint, no administration-time stock decrement.

WardDayAccrualJob as Spring @Scheduled+ShedLock midnight cron (0 5 0 * * *), one WARD InvoiceLine per calendar night — PHANTOM mechanism. Zero scheduling infra in HMSCLEAN2 (confirmed). Legacy accrual is a 5-min polling Thread keyed on rolling-24h-from-openedAt. The cron+ShedLock model is NET-NEW (acceptable per ADR-0018) but must be CR-tagged, not exact-process.

Accrual idempotency via unique (admission_uid, business_date) + ON CONFLICT, exactly one line per calendar night — DRIFT. Legacy keys on rolling-24h-from-openedAt, NOT a calendar boundary; the calendar-date model can produce a different night count for admissions crossing midnight. Golden-master must reconcile elapsed-24h legacy output or document the variance as an accepted deviation.

Ward price via ServicePrice(kind=WARD) cited to ADR-0009 with simple plan-keyed lookup + cash fallback — DRIFT. Legacy insurance ward pricing is a max-price-OR-exact-plan-match loop, IGNORES the active flag, and creates a 'Top up' supplementary bill for the price difference. ServicePrice(kind=WARD) is acceptable as the new home only if it faithfully re-homes WardType.price (cash) AND the WardTypeInsurancePlan selection logic + top-up split (currently a deferred billing-engine gap per PriceLookupImpl :30-38).

Typed DischargePlan.kind discriminator {DISCHARGE,DECEASED,REFERRAL} on one polymorphic entity — DRIFT/PHANTOM. Legacy has THREE separate tables; no kind column. Admission typed states ADMITTED -> DISCHARGED/DECEASED/REFERRED/TRANSFERRED — DRIFT. Legacy free-text values are PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT. Admission fields billsCleared (dispatcher-owned flag) + businessDayId — PHANTOM. No billsCleared field; clearance is recomputed live (UNPAID/VERIFIED) at summary time; no business-day field on Admission. A denormalised flag is a NET-NEW CR (and must compute 'cleared' as no bill UNPAID/VERIFIED, not balance==0).

CASH-only discharge block + ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING 409 — DRIFT. Behaviour (hard reject) is real but the mechanism (denormalised flag set by dispatcher) is invented and the CASH-only scoping is wrong: legacy gates ALL admissions across all three dispositions; VERIFIED as well as UNPAID counts as outstanding.

Six nursing aggregates incl. first-class FluidBalanceEntry + CareActivityEntry (with type enum) — DRIFT. Legacy has FIVE entities; fluid-balance and care-activity are free-text columns on PatientNursingChart. Splitting them is a CR-tracked re-model; the CareActivityType enum collapses the legacy random/full blood-sugar distinction.
NursingProgressNote.kind {DOCTOR,NURSING,OBSERVATION,HANDOVER} — PHANTOM. Single free-text 'note', no category column.

DressingChartEntry immutable + WoundStatus enum (CLEAN/HEALING/.../NECROTIC/DEHISCED) — PHANTOM. No WoundStatus, no wound field; it is a billing record with mandatory PatientBill + ProcedureType. The doc omits the entire billing side-effect and credit-note reversal, and 'immutable' contradicts the 24h-delete-with-reversal window.
NursingCarePlanItem per-problem with ACTIVE->RESOLVED/CANCELLED lifecycle — DRIFT. Legacy is one snapshot record with four free-text strings, no status field.

AdmissionVitalsEntry immutable append-only series, BP split systolic/diastolic — DRIFT. Legacy is a single-row upsert per context with EMPTY->PENDING->SUBMITTED submit state machine; 'pressure' is one field; restricting vitals to admission-only narrows legacy (vitals support consultation/nonConsultation too).

Inc-01 privilege set ADMISSION-DISCHARGE / NURSING-CHART-WRITE / MAR-WRITE / DISCHARGE-PLAN-APPROVE seeded — DRIFT/PHANTOM. Only ADMISSION-CREATE is legacy-derivable; the rest have no legacy basis and (worse) the legacy purge loop would auto-DELETE *-WRITE/*-DISCHARGE codes (operation suffix not in the canonical set). DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE is a PHANTOM tied to the phantom second-approver gate. Requires a CR-approved IAM convention decision before any *-WRITE/*-DISCHARGE code can persist.

Second-approver discharge gate approvedBy != createdBy (M17) + SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN 422 — PHANTOM. Legacy approver is ALWAYS copied from the creator; requiring them to differ would forbid the only path legacy executes. NET-NEW segregation-of-duties CR, not a 'missing gate' fix.

Deceased patient guard on admit()/book() (DISCH-4) setting Patient.deceased=true and rejecting with PATIENT_DECEASED — PHANTOM. No deceased boolean (it is PatientType.DECEASED string, CR-05), no PATIENT_DECEASED error code, no admit-time deceased check in legacy. NET-NEW safety CR. (Legacy DOES already block re-admission while a PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission is open and blocks outpatient consultation while admitted — preserve those.)

Approver worklist GET .../discharge-plans?status=PENDING (PENDING-only, gated by DISCHARGE_PLAN_APPROVE) — DRIFT. Legacy load_discharge_list returns status IN (PENDING,APPROVED) in the CASHIER/payments lane with NO active @PreAuthorize. PENDING-only + distinct-approver framing is net-new.

DISCHARGE kind 'requires' history/investigation/management/recommendations — DRIFT. Legacy performs no required-field validation at create for discharge (contrast DeceasedNote which DOES validate patientSummary+causeOfDeath). Field name 'recommendations' drifts from legacy 'generalRecommendation'.

CAVEAT: drop the 'DISCH-5 gap / legacy was free-text' premise (legacy already uses the FK); and in HMSCLEAN2 the ExternalMedicalProvider masterdata entity DOES NOT EXIST — clinical ReferralPlan (V28) carries external_medical_provider_uid as a MANDATORY LOOSE ref with NO FK (table not built). WardBed released to EMPTY on terminal transition (ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS): legacy sets WardBed=EMPTY on signout (deceased frees at HELD). CORRECTION: legacy NEVER closes the AdmissionBed at discharge (leaves final OPENED) — decide whether to reproduce the leak or CR-fix; do not silently merge WardBed and AdmissionBed.

newDrift: ACTIVATION GATE (PENDING-vs-IN-PROCESS) entirely omitted by the doc (HIGH RISK): the doc's single ADMITTED state erases legacy payment-gated activation. WARD-BILL STATUS DISTINCTION load-bearing: admission-time ward bill is UNPAID, accrued bills are VERIFIED, insurance COVERED. The discharge gate keys on BOTH UNPAID and VERIFIED, so uniform-line modelling breaks the gate.

ADMISSIONBED LEDGER unrepresented: AdmissionBed (status OPENED/CLOSED, @OneToOne PatientBill, openedAt/closedAt) backs ward-day billing and accrual idempotency. 3 years of AdmissionBed rows must migrate and reconcile against PatientBill (flag for data-architect/data-migration).

INPATIENT/NURSE WORKLISTS ARE STATUS FILTERS NOT RBAC GATES: get_doctor_inpatient_list = findAllByStatusIn(['IN-PROCESS','STOPPED']); get_nurse_inpatient_list = findAllByStatus('IN-PROCESS'); no @PreAuthorize. A role-scoped nurse worklist is a net-new access layer (CR note).
synthesis.recommendedScope:
SPLIT inc-07 into three sequential sub-increments, mirroring the inc-08 a/b/c pattern, with the architecture prerequisites landed first.

ARCHITECTURE PREREQUISITES (land before 07a code):
1. ServiceKind extension decision (CR): there is no CONSUMABLE kind and WARD is per-STAY only. Decide (a) charge inpatient consumables as MEDICINE (reuses existing kind, matches legacy 'Medication' billItem + MedicineInsurancePlan path most closely), or (b) add ServiceKind.CONSUMABLE — a new pricing kind requiring a CR + service_prices CHECK change. Recommend (a) as exact-process baseline since legacy prices consumables off Medicine.price/MedicineInsurancePlan.
2. Inpatient stock-decrement seam decision (CR/ADR): for the EXACT-PROCESS baseline, legacy consumable issue touches NO stock — so the baseline needs NO new decrement seam at all (consumable = billing-only). A published inpatient stock-decrement seam over the inc-08 engine (a new pharmacy/api or inventory/api CONSUMABLE_ISSUE-flavored decrementFefo mirroring PharmacyStockCredit) is required ONLY for the NET-NEW 'consumables draw from stock' CR — keep it out of the exact-process scope.
3. billsCleared dispatcher decision (ADR): the discharge gate must reject if ANY admission bill is UNPAID or VERIFIED, across discharge/referral/deceased, for ALL admissions. Either recompute live (legacy-faithful, recommended) or add a new BillingQueries.outstanding/billsCleared(patientUid|admissionUid) query (no such query exists today) computed as 'no bill UNPAID/VERIFIED' — plus a new inpatient settlement listener (ConsultationSettlementListener ignores ward/registration bills).
4. ErrorCodes: add what is genuinely needed by the exact-process baseline. INSUFFICIENT_STOCK already exists (alias of STOCK_INSUFFICIENT). PATIENT_DECEASED, ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING, SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN are absent and tie to NET-NEW CRs — add only if/when those CRs are approved.
5. Admission status enum (ADR): a typed AdmissionStatus is acceptable, mapped 1:1 to legacy PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT (preserve the activation distinction and HELD; no TRANSFERRED).

07a — ADMISSION + DISCHARGE + WARD (exact-process baseline): two-phase admission lifecycle (PENDING bed WAITING + UNPAID ward bill; full-insurance-cover activates; payment-driven activation promotes PENDING->IN-PROCESS + bed OCCUPIED + signs out IN-PROCESS consultations); admit guards (existing open admission, bed active+EMPTY); consultation->admission trigger (inc-07 owns it); WardType.price cash + WardTypeInsurancePlan max-price/exact-match + TOP-UP bill (or its service_prices re-home); AdmissionBed ledger; the three separate disposition entities (DischargePlan/ReferralPlan/DeceasedNote, admission AND consultation paths); the hard bills-cleared gate (UNPAID/VERIFIED, all three dispositions, approve-on-success side effect); bed/patient resets; consultation-blocked-while-admitted guard. PARK as CRs: WardTransfer, second-approver gate, deceased-readmit guard, business-day-keyed accrual idempotency.

07b — NURSING CHARTS (exact-process baseline): FIVE entities (PatientVital single-row upsert + EMPTY->PENDING->SUBMITTED submit machine + context-exclusivity gate + request->GeneralExamination; PatientNursingChart with fluid-balance + care-activity as columns; PatientNursingCarePlan four strings; PatientNursingProgressNote single note; PatientDressingChart as a BILLING record with full bill trichotomy + Dressing marker guard + credit-note reversal on delete). Admission-only inserts (except vitals), nurse-required, admission-status gate, 24h edit/delete window. PARK as CRs: FluidBalance/CareActivity as first-class entities, ProgressNote.kind, WoundStatus, care-plan lifecycle, vitals append-only series + BP split.

07c — CONSUMABLE + WARD-CHARGE ACCRUAL: consumable issue (billing-only, three-way bill status, invoiceDetail qty=1 quirk, consumable-registration guard, admission-status gate, delete/credit-note path) as exact-process baseline with NO stock effect. Ward-day accrual: build the NET-NEW @Scheduled+ShedLock cron job (ADR-0018) as an EXPLICITLY CR-tagged process improvement that REPRODUCES the legacy per-24h accrual TOTAL/status semantics (first bill UNPAID, accrued bills VERIFIED, accrual stops on status change), with golden-master reconciled against the legacy rolling-24h Thread output (document the midnight-vs-24h timing variance as an accepted deviation). Requires net-new scheduling infrastructure (none exists) + a CR.

RBAC: gating the inpatient surface is entirely NET-NEW (legacy was ungated). Keep ADMISSION-CREATE (real code); register all other inpatient privilege codes as a CR-approved RBAC-hardening with an explicit IAM operation-vocabulary decision (the legacy purge loop rejects *-WRITE/*-DISCHARGE). Requires security-architect + engagement-lead sign-off before @PreAuthorize gates assert any new code.
openQuestions: MAR-as-new-feature: approve the closed-loop MedicationAdministration aggregate (routeUid, administeredAt, doseGiven, patientResponse) as a NET-NEW clinical-safety CR distinct from exact-process? Legacy has no MAR; if not approved, inpatient med tracking is only the legacy free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing note (which inc-07 still owns the deferred write path for, with the GIVEN + admission-IN-PROCESS + nurse guards).

Ward-day accrual mechanism: ratify the NET-NEW @Scheduled+ShedLock midnight-cron model (ADR-0018) over reproducing the legacy rolling-24h-from-openedAt polling Thread? If ratified, accept the documented timing variance for admissions crossing midnight (cron may produce a different night count) and confirm the golden-master reconciles against legacy elapsed-24h output, not calendar nights. Also confirm whether to add the scheduling infrastructure at all (none exists today).

Pessimistic lock vs inc-08's parked CR-08-Q4: inc-07 inherits @Version-only concurrency (no pessimistic lock anywhere).
